    "sandbox_policy": {
      "type": "workspace-write",
      "network_access": false,
      "exclude_tmpdir_env_var": false,
      "exclude_slash_tmp": false
    },
    "model": "gpt-5.3-codex",
    "personality": "pragmatic",
    "collaboration_mode": {
      "mode": "plan",
      "settings": {
        "model": "gpt-5.3-codex",
        "reasoning_effort": "medium",
        "developer_instructions": "# Plan Mode (Conversational)\n\nYou work in 3 phases, and you should *chat your way* to a great plan before finalizing it. A great plan is very detailed—intent- and implementation-wise—so that it can be handed to another engineer or agent to be implemented right away. It must be **decision complete**, where the implementer does not need to make any decisions.\n\n## Mode rules (strict)\n\nYou are in **Plan Mode** until a developer message explicitly ends it.\n\nPlan Mode is not changed by user intent, tone, or imperative language. If a user asks for execution while still in Plan Mode, treat it as a request to **plan the execution**, not perform it.\n\n## Plan Mode vs update_plan tool\n\nPlan Mode is a collaboration mode that can involve requesting user input and eventually issuing a `<proposed_plan>` block.\n\nSeparately, `update_plan` is a checklist/progress/TODOs tool; it does not enter or exit Plan Mode. Do not confuse it with Plan mode or try to use it while in Plan mode. If you try to use `update_plan` in Plan mode, it will return an error.\n\n## Execution vs. mutation in Plan Mode\n\nYou may explore and execute **non-mutating** actions that improve the plan. You must not perform **mutating** actions.\n\n### Allowed (non-mutating, plan-improving)\n\nActions that gather truth, reduce ambiguity, or validate feasibility without changing repo-tracked state. Examples:\n\n* Reading or searching files, configs, schemas, types, manifests, and docs\n* Static analysis, inspection, and repo exploration\n* Dry-run style commands when they do not edit repo-tracked files\n* Tests, builds, or checks that may write to caches or build artifacts (for example, `target/`, `.cache/`, or snapshots) so long as they do not edit repo-tracked files\n\n### Not allowed (mutating, plan-executing)\n\nActions that implement the plan or change repo-tracked state. Examples:\n\n* Editing or writing files\n* Running formatters or linters that rewrite files\n* Applying patches, migrations, or codegen that updates repo-tracked files\n* Side-effectful commands whose purpose is to carry out the plan rather than refine it\n\nWhen in doubt: if the action would reasonably be described as \"doing the work\" rather than \"planning the work,\" do not do it.\n\n## PHASE 1 — Ground in the environment (explore first, ask second)\n\nBegin by grounding yourself in the actual environment. Eliminate unknowns in the prompt by discovering facts, not by asking the user. Resolve all questions that can be answered through exploration or inspection. Identify missing or ambiguous details only if they cannot be derived from the environment. Silent exploration between turns is allowed and encouraged.\n\nBefore asking the user any question, perform at least one targeted non-mutating exploration pass (for example: search relevant files, inspect likely entrypoints/configs, confirm current implementation shape), unless no local environment/repo is available.\n\nException: you may ask clarifying questions about the user's prompt before exploring, ONLY if there are obvious ambiguities or contradictions in the prompt itself. However, if ambiguity might be resolved by exploring, always prefer exploring first.\n\nDo not ask questions that can be answered from the repo or system (for example, \"where is this struct?\" or \"which UI component should we use?\" when exploration can make it clear). Only ask once you have exhausted reasonable non-mutating exploration.\n\n## PHASE 2 — Intent chat (what they actually want)\n\n* Keep asking until you can clearly state: goal + success criteria, audience, in/out of scope, constraints, current state, and the key preferences/tradeoffs.\n* Bias toward questions over guessing: if any high-impact ambiguity remains, do NOT plan yet—ask.\n\n## PHASE 3 — Implementation chat (what/how we’ll build)\n\n* Once intent is stable, keep asking until the spec is decision complete: approach, interfaces (APIs/schemas/I/O), data flow, edge cases/failure modes, testing + acceptance criteria, rollout/monitoring, and any migrations/compat constraints.\n\n## Asking questions\n\nCritical rules:\n\n* Strongly prefer using the `request_user_input` tool to ask any questions.\n* Offer only meaningful multiple‑choice options; don’t include filler choices that are obviously wrong or irrelevant.\n* In rare cases where an unavoidable, important question can’t be expressed with reasonable multiple‑choice options (due to extreme ambiguity), you may ask it directly without the tool.\n\nYou SHOULD ask many questions, but each question must:\n\n* materially change the spec/plan, OR\n* confirm/lock an assumption, OR\n* choose between meaningful tradeoffs.\n* not be answerable by non-mutating commands.\n\nUse the `request_user_input` tool only for decisions that materially change the plan, for confirming important assumptions, or for information that cannot be discovered via non-mutating exploration.\n\n## Two kinds of unknowns (treat differently)\n\n1. **Discoverable facts** (repo/system truth): explore first.\n\n   * Before asking, run targeted searches and check likely sources of truth (configs/manifests/entrypoints/schemas/types/constants).\n   * Ask only if: multiple plausible candidates; nothing found but you need a missing identifier/context; or ambiguity is actually product intent.\n   * If asking, present concrete candidates (paths/service names) + recommend one.\n   * Never ask questions you can answer from your environment (e.g., “where is this struct”).\n\n2. **Preferences/tradeoffs** (not discoverable): ask early.\n\n   * These are intent or implementation preferences that cannot be derived from exploration.\n   * Provide 2–4 mutually exclusive options + a recommended default.\n   * If unanswered, proceed with the recommended option and record it as an assumption in the final plan.\n\n## Finalization rule\n\nOnly output the final plan when it is decision complete and leaves no decisions to the implementer.\n\nWhen you present the official plan, wrap it in a `<proposed_plan>` block so the client can render it specially:\n\n1) The opening tag must be on its own line.\n2) Start the plan content on the next line (no text on the same line as the tag).\n3) The closing tag must be on its own line.\n4) Use Markdown inside the block.\n5) Keep the tags exactly as `<proposed_plan>` and `</proposed_plan>` (do not translate or rename them), even if the plan content is in another language.\n\nExample:\n\n<proposed_plan>\nplan content\n</proposed_plan>\n\nplan content should be human and agent digestible. The final plan must be plan-only and include:\n\n* A clear title\n* A brief summary section\n* Important changes or additions to public APIs/interfaces/types\n* Test cases and scenarios\n* Explicit assumptions and defaults chosen where needed\n\nDo not ask \"should I proceed?\" in the final output. The user can easily switch out of Plan mode and request implementation if you have included a `<proposed_plan>` block in your response. Alternatively, they can decide to stay in Plan mode and continue refining the plan.\n\nOnly produce at most one `<proposed_plan>` block per turn, and only when you are presenting a complete spec.\n\nIf the user stays in Plan mode and asks for revisions after a prior `<proposed_plan>`, any new `<proposed_plan>` must be a complete replacement.\n"
      }
    },
    "effort": "medium",
    "summary": "none",
    "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md — Reference Index\n\n> Every agent working in this codebase must read the files listed below before producing\n> any output. Each file governs a specific concern. Read the ones that apply to your task.\n\n---\n\n## Reference Files\n\n| File | Governs |\n|---|---|\n| `PRODUCT.md` | The 7 modules, what each one owns, dependency order, inter-module event map, Eventbrite ACL facade names, hard architectural rules, and where new code goes. Read this before touching any module. |\n| `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` | Eventbrite integration architecture: ACL facades, all FR flows (planned vs. implemented), critical constraints (no user/order creation, no single-order cancel, no seat locking), workarounds. Read before designing any Eventbrite feature. |\n| `docs/MODULE_STRUCTURE_TEMPLATE.md` | Canonical folder and package layout for every module. The exact location of every layer: `api/controller/`, `api/dto/`, `domain/`, `service/`, `repository/`, `mapper/`, `event/published/`, `event/listener/`, `statemachine/`, `exception/`. Anti-patterns list. Read this before writing any class. |\n| `agents/SPEC_GENERATOR.md` | How to produce a `SPEC.md` for any feature. Eight confirmation-gated stages with module context and Eventbrite constraints built-in. Requirements → Domain Model → Architecture & File Structure → DB Schema → API → Business Logic → Error Handling → Tests. Read this before speccing any feature. |\n| `agents/CODING_AGENT.md` | Spring Boot 3.5.11 / Java 21 coding standards. Domain behaviour, service orchestration, mapper rules, controller rules, event rules, 10 Eventbrite ACL facades, inter-module communication (REST + Spring Events), admin/ special rules, state machine, Spring AI. Read this before writing any production code. |\n| `agents/TEST_AGENT.md` | Test agent responsibilities and best practices. Builds tests from `SPEC_*.md` Stage 8, executes tests, fixes test/code issues caused by behavior mismatches, and writes timestamped logs under `test_result_folder/`. Read this before writing or running tests. |\n| `docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md` | Four-layer test structure: `domain/` (unit), `service/` (Mockito), `api/` (@WebMvcTest), `arch/` (ArchUnit enforcing 10 hard rules + Eventbrite constraints). All rule examples, happy path, negative path, exception patterns. Read before writing any test. |\n| `mock-eventbrite-api/README.md` | **Mock Eventbrite Service:** Python FastAPI in-memory mock of Eventbrite API v3. Used for local development and testing. Exposes all 10 ACL facade endpoints without hitting production Eventbrite. See setup instructions below. |\n\n---\n\n## Module Quick Reference\n\nFull detail for each module is in `PRODUCT.md`. This table is a lookup only.\n\n| Module | Owns | Eventbrite ACL Facades Used |\n|---|---|---|\n| `discovery-catalog` | Events, Venues, Cities, catalog sync | `EbEventSyncService`, `EbTicketService` |\n| `scheduling` | Show slots, conflict validation | `EbEventSyncService` |\n| `identity` | Users, JWT, profiles, preferences, wallet | `EbAttendeeService` |\n| `booking-inventory` | Seats, SeatMap, Cart, Pricing, Seat Lock State Machine | `EbTicketService` |\n| `payments-ticketing` | Bookings, Payments, E-Tickets, Cancellations, Refunds, Wallet | `EbOrderService`, `EbAttendeeService`, `EbRefundService` |\n| `promotions` | Coupons, Promotions, Eligibility rules | `EbDiscountSyncService` |\n| `engagement` | Reviews, Moderation, AI Chatbot (`service/chatbot/`), RAG pipeline | `EbEventSyncService` |\n| `admin/` | No domain ownership. Orchestration and aggregation only. | — |\n| `shared/` | All external ACL facades, JWT, base classes, common DTOs, configs | — |\n\n---\n\n## Hard Rules — Never Violate\n\nCanonical numbering and wording are owned by `PRODUCT.md` Section 9. These shorthand rules apply to every task without exception.\n\n1. No module imports another module's `@Service`, `@Entity`, or `@Repository`.\n  - Exception: `admin/` MAY import module `@Service` beans for READ operations only; all writes MUST go through the owning module REST API.\n2. No module calls Eventbrite, Razorpay, or OpenAI HTTP directly — only through `shared/` ACL facades.\n3. ACL facade names are exactly as listed below — never invent new ones.\n4. Spring Events carry only primitives, IDs, enums, and value objects — never `@Entity` objects.\n5. One `GlobalExceptionHandler` in `shared/common/exception/` — never `@ControllerAdvice` in a module.\n6. All field mapping goes in `mapper/` using MapStruct — never inside a service or controller.\n7. `admin/` owns no `@Entity` and no database table.\n8. `shared/` has no dependency on any module.\n9. `app/` has zero business logic.\n10. Dependency direction is strictly downward — no circular imports between modules.\n\n---\n\n## Eventbrite ACL Facade Names (HARD RULE #3)\nRead the docs/eventbriteapiv3public.apib for detail API Endpoint with schema, Response, Request and errors\n\nAll 10 facades are in `shared/eventbrite/service/`. These are the ONLY things modules call for Eventbrite:\n\n1. **EbEventSyncService** — Create, update, publish, cancel events; pull org event catalog\n2. **EbVenueService** — Create and update venues; list org venues\n3. **EbScheduleService** — Create recurring event schedules and occurrences\n4. **EbTicketService** — Create/update ticket classes; manage inventory; check availability\n5. **EbCapacityService** — Retrieve and update event capacity tiers\n6. **EbOrderService** — Read orders post-checkout (NOT creation — JS SDK widget only)\n7. **EbAttendeeService** — List attendees; verify attendance for reviews\n8. **EbDiscountSyncService** — Create, update, delete discount codes\n9. **EbRefundService** — Read refund status only (NO submission API — workaround required)\n10. **EbWebhookService** — Mock/testing only; not used in production registration flow\n\n**See `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` for which module uses which facade and what gaps exist.**\n\n---\n\n## Module Dependency Order (HARD RULE #10)\n\n```\nshared/               <- foundational; no dependencies\n  |\n  v\ndiscovery-catalog    <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nscheduling           <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\nidentity             <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nbooking-inventory    <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\n  |\n  v\npayments-ticketing   <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\npromotions           <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nengagement           <- depends on shared + payments-ticketing + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nadmin/               <- thin orchestration; reads from all, writes via REST APIs\n  |\n  v\napp/                 <- entry point only; zero business logic\n```\n\n**Golden Rule:** Module at level N can depend on level N-1 or lower (or `shared/`) only. Never import from sibling or downstream modules.\n\n**See `PRODUCT.md` Section 2 for detailed rationale.**\n\n\n## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \"how do I do X\", \"find a skill for X\", \"is there a skill that can...\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. (file: /home/dell/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md)\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.",
    "truncation_policy": {
      "mode": "tokens",
      "limit": 10000
    }
  }
}

{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T13:12:50.107Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "message",
    "role": "user",
    "content": [
      {
        "type": "input_text",
        "text": "You are a senior Java architect working on a Spring Boot 3.5.11 modular monolith \n(Java 21). The system has the following modules in strict dependency order:\n\nshared/ → discovery-catalog → scheduling → identity → booking-inventory \n→ payments-ticketing → promotions → engagement → admin/ → app/\n\nHARD RULES (never violate):\n1. No module imports another module's @Entity, @Repository, or @Service directly\n2. Cross-module communication is either: shared interface (DI), REST call, \n   or Spring ApplicationEvent\n3. shared/ has zero dependency on any module — any module can import from shared/\n4. Spring Events carry only primitives, IDs, enums, value objects — never @Entity\n5. One GlobalExceptionHandler in shared/common/exception/ only\n6. Dependency direction is strictly downward — no circular imports\n\nCURRENT STATE:\nbooking-inventory makes two HTTP REST calls to scheduling on the user hot path:\n\nCall 1 — Slot Summary Read\n  From:    booking-inventory/service/CartService.java\n           booking-inventory/service/SeatAvailabilityService.java  \n  To:      GET /api/v1/scheduling/slots/{slotId}\n  Returns: slotId, status (ACTIVE/DRAFT/etc), ebEventId, seatingMode, orgId, venueId\n  Called:  On every GET /available-seats, every POST /cart/add-seat, \n           every POST /cart/confirm\n  Current: SchedulingSlotClient.java using RestTemplate\n\nCall 2 — Pricing Tier Read  \n  From:    booking-inventory/service/CartPricingService.java\n           booking-inventory/service/SeatProvisioningService.java\n  To:      GET /api/v1/scheduling/slots/{slotId}/pricing-tiers\n  Returns: List of (tierId, tierName, priceAmount, currency, quota, tierType,\n           ebTicketClassId, ebInventoryTierId, groupDiscountThreshold, \n           groupDiscountPercent)\n  Called:  On every POST /cart/add-seat, every POST /cart/confirm,\n           once at slot activation (SeatProvisioningService)\n  Current: SchedulingSlotClient.java using RestTemplate\n\nTASK:\nConvert both HTTP calls to shared interface approach. Produce ALL of the following:\n\n1. shared/common/service/SlotSummaryReader.java\n   Interface with method signature(s) for slot summary read.\n   Return type must be a new DTO: SlotSummaryDto\n   Include all fields booking-inventory needs.\n\n2. shared/common/service/SlotPricingReader.java  \n   Interface with method signature(s) for pricing tier read.\n   Return type must be a new DTO: PricingTierDto (and a wrapper \n   SlotPricingResponse if needed)\n   Include all fields booking-inventory needs including discount fields.\n\n3. shared/common/dto/SlotSummaryDto.java\n   Java record. All fields booking-inventory currently extracts from \n   the REST response.\n\n4. shared/common/dto/PricingTierDto.java\n   Java record. All fields including ebTicketClassId, groupDiscountThreshold,\n   groupDiscountPercent.\n\n5. scheduling/service/SlotSummaryReaderImpl.java\n   Implements SlotSummaryReader.\n   Annotated @Service.\n   Reads from ShowSlot entity via ShowSlotRepository (scheduling owns this).\n   Maps to SlotSummaryDto.\n   Throws ResourceNotFoundException (from shared/common/exception/) if not found.\n\n6. scheduling/service/SlotPricingReaderImpl.java\n   Implements SlotPricingReader.\n   Annotated @Service.\n   Reads from ShowSlotPricingTier entity via ShowSlotPricingTierRepository.\n   Maps to List<PricingTierDto>.\n   Throws ResourceNotFoundException if slot not found.\n   Returns empty list (not null) if slot has no pricing tiers.\n\n7. booking-inventory/service/CartService.java (updated)\n   Replace SchedulingSlotClient HTTP calls with SlotSummaryReader injection.\n   Keep all existing logic — only the data fetch mechanism changes.\n   Show constructor injection of SlotSummaryReader (interface, not impl).\n\n8. booking-inventory/service/CartPricingService.java (updated)\n   Replace SchedulingSlotClient HTTP calls with SlotPricingReader injection.\n   Keep Redis cache logic: check Redis key pricing:slot:{slotId} first,\n   call interface only on cache miss, write result to Redis with 10-min TTL.\n   Show full cache-aside pattern with the interface call on miss.\n\n9. booking-inventory/service/SeatAvailabilityService.java (updated)\n   Replace SchedulingSlotClient HTTP calls with SlotSummaryReader injection.\n\n10. Updated ArchUnit test rules\n    Replace: booking-inventory must not import scheduling.*\n    With:    booking-inventory must not import scheduling.domain.*\n             booking-inventory must not import scheduling.repository.*\n             booking-inventory must not import scheduling.service.*\n             booking-inventory MAY import shared.common.service.SlotSummaryReader\n             booking-inventory MAY import shared.common.service.SlotPricingReader\n             booking-inventory MAY import shared.common.dto.*\n\n11. Unit test for SlotSummaryReaderImpl\n    Test: returns correct DTO when slot exists\n    Test: throws ResourceNotFoundException when slot not found\n    Test: maps all fields correctly including ebEventId and seatingMode enum\n\n12. Unit test for SlotPricingReaderImpl  \n    Test: returns correct list when pricing tiers exist\n    Test: returns empty list when no tiers configured for slot\n    Test: maps groupDiscountThreshold and groupDiscountPercent correctly\n\n13. Unit test for CartPricingService cache-aside\n    Test: returns cached result on Redis hit (no interface call)\n    Test: calls SlotPricingReader on Redis miss and caches result\n    Test: Redis failure falls back to direct interface call (no exception thrown)\n\nCONSTRAINTS FOR ALL OUTPUT:\n- Use constructor injection everywhere — never @Autowired field injection\n- Use Java records for all DTOs\n- SlotSummaryReader and SlotPricingReader interfaces go in shared/common/service/\n- DTOs go in shared/common/dto/\n- Implementations go in scheduling/service/\n- No @Transactional on the reader implementations unless a write occurs \n  (reads only — use @Transactional(readOnly = true))\n- ResourceNotFoundException must be imported from shared/common/exception/ \n  not redefined\n- SchedulingSlotClient.java can be deleted after this refactor — \n  confirm it has no other callers first\n- The REST endpoints GET /api/v1/scheduling/slots/{slotId} and \n  GET /api/v1/scheduling/slots/{slotId}/pricing-tiers must be KEPT \n  for admin/ module usage and external API consumers — \n  do not delete the controllers\n- seatingMode field must use the SeatingMode enum \n  (shared/common/enums/SeatingMode.java — GA, RESERVED) not a raw String\n- All monetary amounts use the Money value object \n  (shared/common/domain/Money.java) not raw BigDecimal\n\nOUTPUT FORMAT:\nFor each file produce:\n  Full file path\n  Complete file content\n  A 2-line note explaining what changed and why\n\nDo not produce partial files. Every file must be complete and compilable.\nDo not add any new dependencies to pom.xml — \nSpring Data JPA, Spring Context, and Lombok/MapStruct are already present."
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

Produce ALL of the following:\n\n1. shared/common/service/SlotSummaryReader.java\n   Interface with method signature(s) for slot summary read.\n   Return type must be a new DTO: SlotSummaryDto\n   Include all fields booking-inventory needs.\n\n2. shared/common/service/SlotPricingReader.java  \n   Interface with method signature(s) for pricing tier read.\n   Return type must be a new DTO: PricingTierDto (and a wrapper \n   SlotPricingResponse if needed)\n   Include all fields booking-inventory needs including discount fields.\n\n3. shared/common/dto/SlotSummaryDto.java\n   Java record. All fields booking-inventory currently extracts from \n   the REST response.\n\n4. shared/common/dto/PricingTierDto.java\n   Java record. All fields including ebTicketClassId, groupDiscountThreshold,\n   groupDiscountPercent.\n\n5. scheduling/service/SlotSummaryReaderImpl.java\n   Implements SlotSummaryReader.\n   Annotated @Service.\n   Reads from ShowSlot entity via ShowSlotRepository (scheduling owns this).\n   Maps to SlotSummaryDto.\n   Throws ResourceNotFoundException (from shared/common/exception/) if not found.\n\n6. scheduling/service/SlotPricingReaderImpl.java\n   Implements SlotPricingReader.\n   Annotated @Service.\n   Reads from ShowSlotPricingTier entity via ShowSlotPricingTierRepository.\n   Maps to List<PricingTierDto>.\n   Throws ResourceNotFoundException if slot not found.\n   Returns empty list (not null) if slot has no pricing tiers.\n\n7. booking-inventory/service/CartService.java (updated)\n   Replace SchedulingSlotClient HTTP calls with SlotSummaryReader injection.\n   Keep all existing logic — only the data fetch mechanism changes.\n   Show constructor injection of SlotSummaryReader (interface, not impl).\n\n8. booking-inventory/service/CartPricingService.java (updated)\n   Replace SchedulingSlotClient HTTP calls with SlotPricingReader injection.\n   Keep Redis cache logic: check Redis key pricing:slot:{slotId} first,\n   call interface only on cache miss, write result to Redis with 10-min TTL.\n   Show full cache-aside pattern with the interface call on miss.\n\n9. booking-inventory/service/SeatAvailabilityService.java (updated)\n   Replace SchedulingSlotClient HTTP calls with SlotSummaryReader injection.\n\n10. Updated ArchUnit test rules\n    Replace: booking-inventory must not import scheduling.*\n    With:    booking-inventory must not import scheduling.domain.*\n             booking-inventory must not import scheduling.repository.*\n             booking-inventory must not import scheduling.service.*\n             booking-inventory MAY import shared.common.service.SlotSummaryReader\n             booking-inventory MAY import shared.common.service.SlotPricingReader\n             booking-inventory MAY import shared.common.dto.*\n\n11. Unit test for SlotSummaryReaderImpl\n    Test: returns correct DTO when slot exists\n    Test: throws ResourceNotFoundException when slot not found\n    Test: maps all fields correctly including ebEventId and seatingMode enum\n\n12. Unit test for SlotPricingReaderImpl  \n    Test: returns correct list when pricing tiers exist\n    Test: returns empty list when no tiers configured for slot\n    Test: maps groupDiscountThreshold and groupDiscountPercent correctly\n\n13. Unit test for CartPricingService cache-aside\n    Test: returns cached result on Redis hit (no interface call)\n    Test: calls SlotPricingReader on Redis miss and caches result\n    Test: Redis failure falls back to direct interface call (no exception thrown)\n\nCONSTRAINTS FOR ALL OUTPUT:\n- Use constructor injection everywhere — never @Autowired field injection\n- Use Java records for all DTOs\n- SlotSummaryReader and SlotPricingReader interfaces go in shared/common/service/\n- DTOs go in shared/common/dto/\n- Implementations go in scheduling/service/\n- No @Transactional on the reader implementations unless a write occurs \n  (reads only — use @Transactional(readOnly = true))\n- ResourceNotFoundException must be imported from shared/common/exception/ \n  not redefined\n- SchedulingSlotClient.java can be deleted after this refactor — \n  confirm it has no other callers first\n- The REST endpoints GET /api/v1/scheduling/slots/{slotId} and \n  GET /api/v1/scheduling/slots/{slotId}/pricing-tiers must be KEPT \n  for admin/ module usage and external API consumers — \n  do not delete the controllers\n- seatingMode field must use the SeatingMode enum \n  (shared/common/enums/SeatingMode.java — GA, RESERVED) not a raw String\n- All monetary amounts use the Money value object \n  (shared/common/domain/Money.java) not raw BigDecimal\n\nOUTPUT FORMAT:\nFor each file produce:\n  Full file path\n  Complete file content\n  A 2-line note explaining what changed and why\n\nDo not produce partial files. Every file must be complete and compilable.\nDo not add any new dependencies to pom.xml — \nSpring Data JPA, Spring Context, and Lombok/MapStruct are already present."

The system has the following modules in strict dependency order:\n\nshared/ → discovery-catalog → scheduling → identity → booking-inventory \n→ payments-ticketing → promotions → engagement → admin/ → app/\n\nHARD RULES (never violate):\n1. No module imports another module's @Entity, @Repository, or @Service directly\n2. Cross-module communication is either: shared interface (DI), REST call, \n   or Spring ApplicationEvent\n3. shared/ has zero dependency on any module — any module can import from shared/\n4. Spring Events carry only primitives, IDs, enums, value objects — never @Entity\n5. One GlobalExceptionHandler in shared/common/exception/ only\n6. Dependency direction is strictly downward — no circular imports\n\nCURRENT STATE:\nbooking-inventory makes two HTTP REST calls to scheduling on the user hot path:\n\nCall 1 — Slot Summary Read\n  From:    booking-inventory/service/CartService.java\n           booking-inventory/service/SeatAvailabilityService.java  \n  To:      GET /api/v1/scheduling/slots/{slotId}\n  Returns: slotId, status (ACTIVE/DRAFT/etc), ebEventId, seatingMode, orgId, venueId\n  Called:  On every GET /available-seats, every POST /cart/add-seat, \n           every POST /cart/confirm\n  Current: SchedulingSlotClient.java using RestTemplate\n\nCall 2 — Pricing Tier Read  \n  From:    booking-inventory/service/CartPricingService.java\n           booking-inventory/service/SeatProvisioningService.java\n  To:      GET /api/v1/scheduling/slots/{slotId}/pricing-tiers\n  Returns: List of (tierId, tierName, priceAmount, currency, quota, tierType,\n           ebTicketClassId, ebInventoryTierId, groupDiscountThreshold, \n           groupDiscountPercent)\n  Called:  On every POST /cart/add-seat, every POST /cart/confirm,\n           once at slot activation (SeatProvisioningService)\n  Current: SchedulingSlotClient.java using RestTemplate\n\nTASK:\nConvert both HTTP calls to shared interface approach.     "name": "request_user_input",
    "arguments": "{\"questions\":[{\"header\":\"Scope Base\",\"id\":\"scope_base\",\"question\":\"I found a large in-progress refactor already present in `booking-inventory` (many new uncommitted files). For this interface-migration plan, which baseline should I target?\",\"options\":[{\"label\":\"Current working tree (Recommended)\",\"description\":\"Plan against the repo exactly as it is now, and only refactor the scheduling read path to shared readers.\"},{\"label\":\"Clean baseline before refactor\",\"description\":\"Plan as if we first discard/ignore in-progress FR4 files, then apply this change on a minimal baseline.\"},{\"label\":\"Hybrid\",\"description\":\"Plan against current tree but include cleanup/removal of clearly obsolete refactor artifacts as part of this task.\"}]}]}",
    "call_id": "call_F9OgWkrH2wW3XVNEAAy70Aeh"
  }
}

com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.domain.Cart;\nimport com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.domain.CartItem;\nimport com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.domain.GroupDiscountRule;\nimport com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.repository.GroupDiscountRuleRepository;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.common.domain.Money;\nimport java.math.BigDecimal;\nimport java.math.RoundingMode;\nimport java.util.HashMap;\nimport java.util.List;\nimport java.util.Map;\nimport java.util.function.Function;\nimport java.util.stream.Collectors;\nimport org.springframework.stereotype.Service;\n\n@Service\npublic class CartPricingService {\n\n    public record CartPricingResult(\n        Money subtotal,\n        Money discount,\n        Money total,\n        Map<Long, Money> itemDiscounts\n    ) {\n    }\n\n    private final GroupDiscountRuleRepository groupDiscountRuleRepository;\n\n    public CartPricingService(GroupDiscountRuleRepository groupDiscountRuleRepository) {\n        this.groupDiscountRuleRepository = groupDiscountRuleRepository;\n    }\n\n    public CartPricingResult recompute(Cart cart, List<CartItem> items) {\n        if (items.isEmpty()) {\n            Money zero = new Money(BigDecimal.ZERO, cart.getDiscountAmount().currency());\n            cart.setDiscountAmount(zero);\n            return new CartPricingResult(zero, zero, zero, Map.of());\n        }\n\n        String currency = items.getFirst().getBasePrice().currency();\n        BigDecimal subtotalAmount = items.stream()\n            .map(this::lineAmount)\n            .reduce(BigDecimal.ZERO, BigDecimal::add);\n\n        Map<Long, List<CartItem>> byTier = items.stream().collect(Collectors.groupingBy(CartItem::getPricingTierId));\n        Map<Long, GroupDiscountRule> rulesByTier = groupDiscountRuleRepository.findByShowSlotId(cart.getShowSlotId()).stream()\n            .collect(Collectors.toMap(GroupDiscountRule::getPricingTierId, Function.identity(), (a, b) -> a));\n\n        BigDecimal totalDiscount = BigDecimal.ZERO;\n        Map<Long, Money> itemDiscounts = new HashMap<>();\n\n        for (Map.Entry<Long, List<CartItem>> entry : byTier.entrySet()) {\n            Long tierId = entry.getKey();\n            List<CartItem> tierItems = entry.getValue();\n            GroupDiscountRule rule = rulesByTier.get(tierId);\n            if (rule == null || rule.getGroupDiscountThreshold() == null || rule.getGroupDiscountPercent() == null) {\n                tierItems.forEach(item -> itemDiscounts.put(item.getId(), new Money(BigDecimal.ZERO, currency)));\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            int count = tierItems.stream().mapToInt(CartItem::getQuantity).sum();\n            if (count < rule.getGroupDiscountThreshold()) {\n                tierItems.forEach(item -> itemDiscounts.put(item.getId(), new Money(BigDecimal.ZERO, currency)));\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            BigDecimal tierSubtotal = tierItems.stream().map(this::lineAmount).reduce(BigDecimal.ZERO, BigDecimal::add);\n            BigDecimal tierDiscount = tierSubtotal\n                .multiply(rule.getGroupDiscountPercent())\n                .divide(new BigDecimal(\"100\"), 2, RoundingMode.HALF_UP);\n            totalDiscount = totalDiscount.add(tierDiscount);\n\n            for (CartItem item : tierItems) {\n                BigDecimal ratio = tierSubtotal.signum() == 0\n                    ? BigDecimal.ZERO\n                    : lineAmount(item).divide(tierSubtotal, 8, RoundingMode.HALF_UP);\n                BigDecimal itemDiscount = tierDiscount.multiply(ratio).setScale(2, RoundingMode.HALF_UP);\n                itemDiscounts.put(item.getId(), new Money(itemDiscount, currency));\n            }\n        }\n\n        Money subtotal = new Money(subtotalAmount.setScale(2, RoundingMode.HALF_UP), currency);\n        Money discount = new Money(totalDiscount.setScale(2, RoundingMode.HALF_UP), currency);\n        Money total = new Money(subtotal.amount().subtract(discount.amount()).setScale(2, RoundingMode.HALF_UP), currency);\n        cart.setDiscountAmount(discount);\n\n        return new CartPricingResult(subtotal, discount, total, itemDiscounts);\n    }\n\n    private BigDecimal lineAmount(CartItem item) {\n        return item.getBasePrice().amount().multiply(BigDecimal.valueOf(item.getQuantity()));\n    }\n}\npackage com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.service;\n\nimport com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.api.dto.request.AddSeatRequest;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.eventbrite.service.EbTokenStore;\nimport java.math.BigDecimal;\nimport java.time.Duration;\nimport java.time.Instant;\nimport java.util.ArrayList;\nimport java.util.HashMap;\nimport java.util.List;\nimport java.util.Map;\nimport java.util.Objects;\nimport java.util.Set;\nimport java.util.function.Function;\nimport java.util.stream.Collectors;\nimport org.springframework.context.ApplicationEventPublisher;\nimport org.springframework.dao.DataIntegrityViolationException;\nimport org.springframework.data.redis.core.StringRedisTemplate;\nimport org.springframework.http.HttpStatus;\nimport org.springframework.stereotype.Service;\nimport org.springframework.transaction.annotation.Transactional;\n\n@Service\npublic class CartService {\n\n    private static final Duration SOFT_LOCK_TTL = Duration.ofMinutes(5);\n    private static final Duration HARD_LOCK_TTL = Duration.ofMinutes(30);\n\n    private final CartRepository cartRepository;\n    private final CartItemRepository cartItemRepository;\n    private final SeatRepository seatRepository;\n    private final GaInventoryClaimRepository gaInventoryClaimRepository;\n    private final SeatLockAuditLogRepository seatLockAuditLogRepository;\n    private final SlotValidationService slotValidationService;\n    private final SchedulingSlotClient schedulingSlotClient;\n    private final SeatLockRedisService seatLockRedisService;\n    private final GaInventoryRedisService gaInventoryRedisService;\n    private final CartPricingService cartPricingService;\n    private final ConflictResolutionService conflictResolutionService;\n    private final CartMapper cartMapper;\n    private final EbTicketService ebTicketService;\n    private final EbTokenStore ebTokenStore;\n    private final ApplicationEventPublisher eventPublisher;\n    private final StringRedisTemplate stringRedisTemplate;\n\n    public CartService(\n        CartRepository cartRepository,\n        CartItemRepository cartItemRepository,\n        SeatRepository seatRepository,\n        GaInventoryClaimRepository gaInventoryClaimRepository,\n        SeatLockAuditLogRepository seatLockAuditLogRepository,\n        SlotValidationService slotValidationService,\n        SchedulingSlotClient schedulingSlotClient,\n        SeatLockRedisService seatLockRedisService,\n        GaInventoryRedisService gaInventoryRedisService,\n        CartPricingService cartPricingService,\n        ConflictResolutionService conflictResolutionService,\n        CartMapper cartMapper,\n        EbTicketService ebTicketService,\n        EbTokenStore ebTokenStore,\n        ApplicationEventPublisher eventPublisher,\n        StringRedisTemplate stringRedisTemplate\n    ) {\n        this.cartRepository = cartRepository;\n        this.cartItemRepository = cartItemRepository;\n        this.seatRepository = seatRepository;\n        this.gaInventoryClaimRepository = gaInventoryClaimRepository;\n        this.seatLockAuditLogRepository = seatLockAuditLogRepository;\n        this.slotValidationService = slotValidationService;\n        this.schedulingSlotClient = schedulingSlotClient;\n        this.seatLockRedisService = seatLockRedisService;\n        this.gaInventoryRedisService = gaInventoryRedisService;\n        this.cartPricingService = cartPricingService;\n        this.conflictResolutionService = conflictResolutionService;\n        this.cartMapper = cartMapper;\n        this.ebTicketService = ebTicketService;\n        this.ebTokenStore = ebTokenStore;\n        this.eventPublisher = eventPublisher;\n        this.stringRedisTemplate = stringRedisTemplate;\n    }\n\n    @Transactional\n    public CartResponse addSeat(Long userId, Long orgId, String role, AddSeatRequest request) {\n        slotValidationService.ensureUserActive(role);\n        SchedulingSlotResponse slot = slotValidationService.requireActiveAndSynced(request.slotId());\n        List<SchedulingPricingTierResponse> tiers = schedulingSlotClient.getPricingTiers(request.slotId());\n        Map<Long, SchedulingPricingTierResponse> tierById = tiers.stream().collect(Collectors.toMap(SchedulingPricingTierResponse::id, Function.identity()));\n\n        SchedulingPricingTierResponse tier = tierById.get(request.tierId());\n        if (tier == null) {\n            throw new BusinessRuleException(\"Pricing tier not found for slot\", \"TIER_NOT_FOUND\");\n        }\n        slotValidationService.ensureTierSynced(request.tierId(), tier.ebTicketClassId());\n        assertOrgConnected(orgId);\n\n        Cart cart = getOrCreatePendingCart(userId, slot, orgId, tier.currency());\n\n        if (slot.seatingMode() == com.eventplatform.shared.common.enums.SeatingMode.RESERVED) {\n            if (request.seatId() == null) {\n                throw new BusinessRuleException(\"seatId is required for RESERVED mode\", \"SEAT_ID_REQUIRED\");\n            }\n            addReservedSeat(userId, cart, tierById, request, tier);\n        } else {\n            addGaTier(userId, cart, request, tier);\n        }\n\n        return buildCartResponse(cart, tierById);\n    }\n\n    @Transactional\n    public CartResponse removeItem(Long userId, Long itemId) {\n        CartItem item = cartItemRepository.findByIdAndCartUserId(itemId, userId)\n            .orElseThrow(() -> new CartItemNotFoundException(itemId));\n\n        Cart cart = item.getCart();\n        if (item.getSeatId() != null) {\n            Seat seat = seatRepository.findByIdWithLock(item.getSeatId())\n                .orElseThrow(() -> new BusinessRuleException(\"Seat not found\", \"SEAT_NOT_FOUND\"));\n            if (seat.getLockState() == SeatLockState.HARD_LOCKED\n                || seat.getLockState() == SeatLockState.PAYMENT_PENDING\n                || seat.getLockState() == SeatLockState.CONFIRMED) {\n                throw new BaseException(\"Seat removal not allowed\", \"SEAT_REMOVAL_NOT_ALLOWED\", HttpStatus.CONFLICT,\n                    Map.of(\"lockState\", seat.getLockState().name())) {\n                };\n            }\n            SeatLockState previous = seat.getLockState();\n            seat.release(LockReleaseReason.USER_REMOVED);\n            seatRepository.save(seat);\n            seatLockRedisService.release(seat.getId(), userId);\n            writeAudit(seat, userId, previous, SeatLockState.AVAILABLE, SeatLockEvent.RELEASE, LockReleaseReason.USER_REMOVED.name(), null);\n        } else if (item.getGaClaimId() != null) {\n            gaInventoryClaimRepository.findById(item.getGaClaimId()).ifPresent(claim -> {\n                gaInventoryRedisService.restore(claim.getShowSlotId(), claim.getPricingTierId(), claim.getQuantity());\n                gaInventoryClaimRepository.delete(claim);\n            });\n        }\n\n        cart.removeItem(item);\n        cartItemRepository.delete(item);\n\n        Map<Long, SchedulingPricingTierResponse> tierById = fetchTierMap(cart.getShowSlotId());\n        return buildCartResponse(cart, tierById);\n    }\n\n    @Transactional(readOnly = true)\n    public CartResponse getCart(Long userId, Long cartId) {\n        Cart cart = cartRepository.findById(cartId).orElseThrow(() -> new CartNotFoundException(cartId));\n        if (!Objects.equals(cart.getUserId(), userId)) {\n            throw new CartNotFoundException(cartId);\n        }\n        return buildCartResponse(cart, fetchTierMap(cart.getShowSlotId()));\n    }\n\n    @Transactional\n    public CartResponse confirm(Long userId, Long orgId, ConfirmCartRequest request) {\n        Cart cart = cartRepository.findById(request.cartId()).orElseThrow(() -> new CartNotFoundException(request.cartId()));\n        if (!Objects.equals(cart.getUserId(), userId)) {\n            throw new CartNotFoundException(request.cartId());\n        }\n        cart.ensurePending();\n        if (cart.isExpired(Instant.now())) {\n            throw new CartExpiredException(cart.getId(), cart.getExpiresAt());\n        }\n\n        List<SchedulingPricingTierResponse> tiers = schedulingSlotClient.getPricingTiers(cart.getShowSlotId());\n        Map<Long, SchedulingPricingTierResponse> tierById = tiers.stream().collect(Collectors.toMap(SchedulingPricingTierResponse::id, Function.identity()));\n        SchedulingSlotResponse slot = slotValidationService.requireActiveAndSynced(cart.getShowSlotId());\n\n        Set<Long> expiredSeatIds = cart.getItems().stream()\n            .map(CartItem::getSeatId)\n            .filter(Objects::nonNull)\n            .filter(seatId -> !seatLockRedisService.isOwnedBy(seatId, userId))\n            .collect(Collectors.toSet());\n        if (!expiredSeatIds.isEmpty()) {\n            throw new SoftLockExpiredException(expiredSeatIds);\n        }\n\n        Map<String, Long> ticketClassToTier = tierById.values().stream()\n            .filter(t -> t.ebTicketClassId() != null)\n            .collect(Collectors.toMap(SchedulingPricingTierResponse::ebTicketClassId, SchedulingPricingTierResponse::id, (a, b) -> a));\n\n        for (String ticketClassId : cart.getItems().stream().map(CartItem::getEbTicketClassId).collect(Collectors.toSet())) {\n            EbTicketClassResponse ticketClass;\n            try {\n                ticketClass = ebTicketService.getTicketClass(slot.ebEventId(), ticketClassId);\n            } catch (EbIntegrationException ex) {\n                throw new BaseException(\"Eventbrite unavailable\", \"EB_UNAVAILABLE\", HttpStatus.SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE,\n                    Map.of(\"retryAfterSeconds\", 10)) {\n                };\n            }\n            if (ticketClass != null && \"SOLD_OUT\".equalsIgnoreCase(ticketClass.onSaleStatus())) {\n                Long soldOutTierId = ticketClassToTier.get(ticketClassId);\n                releaseTierLocks(cart, userId, soldOutTierId);\n                List<Map<String, Object>> alternativeTiers = tierById.values().stream()\n                    .filter(t -> !Objects.equals(t.id(), soldOutTierId))\n                    .map(t -> Map.<String, Object>of(\"tierId\", t.id(), \"tierName\", t.name()))\n                    .toList();\n                throw new TierSoldOutException(soldOutTierId, Map.of(\"otherTiers\", alternativeTiers));\n            }\n        }\n\n        for (CartItem item : cart.getItems()) {\n            if (item.getSeatId() != null) {\n                Seat seat = seatRepository.findByIdWithLock(item.getSeatId())\n                    .orElseThrow(() -> new BusinessRuleException(\"Seat not found\", \"SEAT_NOT_FOUND\"));\n                try {\n                    if (!seatLockRedisService.extend(seat.getId(), userId, HARD_LOCK_TTL)) {\n                        throw new HardLockException(\"Failed to extend Redis lock for seat=\" + seat.getId());\n                    }\n                    SeatLockState previous = seat.getLockState();\n                    seat.hardLock(HARD_LOCK_TTL);\n                    seatRepository.save(seat);\n                    writeAudit(seat, userId, previous, SeatLockState.HARD_LOCKED, SeatLockEvent.CONFIRM, null, null);\n                } catch (RuntimeException ex) {\n                    seatLockRedisService.release(seat.getId(), userId);\n                    throw ex;\n                }\n            } else if (item.getGaClaimId() != null) {\n                gaInventoryClaimRepository.findById(item.getGaClaimId()).ifPresent(claim -> {\n                    claim.hardLock(Instant.now().plus(HARD_LOCK_TTL));\n                    gaInventoryClaimRepository.save(claim);\n                });\n            }\n        }\n\n        cart.extendTtl(HARD_LOCK_TTL);\n        cartRepository.save(cart);\n\n        eventPublisher.publishEvent(new CartAssembledEvent(\n            cart.getId(),\n            cart.getShowSlotId(),\n            cart.getUserId(),\n            cart.getOrgId(),\n            slot.ebEventId(),\n            cart.getCouponCode()\n        ));\npackage com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.service;\n\nimport com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.api.dto.response.AvailableSeatResponse;\nimport com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.api.dto.response.AvailableSeatsEnvelopeResponse;\nimport com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.api.dto.response.GaTierAvailabilityResponse;\nimport com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.domain.enums.SeatLockState;\nimport org.springframework.data.redis.core.StringRedisTemplate;\nimport org.springframework.stereotype.Service;\n\n@Service\npublic class SeatAvailabilityService {\n\n    private final SlotValidationService slotValidationService;\n    private final SeatRepository seatRepository;\n    private final SchedulingSlotClient schedulingSlotClient;\n    private final SeatMapper seatMapper;\n    private final GaInventoryRedisService gaInventoryRedisService;\n    private final GaInventoryClaimRepository gaInventoryClaimRepository;\n    private final StringRedisTemplate stringRedisTemplate;\n\n    public SeatAvailabilityService(\n        SlotValidationService slotValidationService,\n        SeatRepository seatRepository,\n        SchedulingSlotClient schedulingSlotClient,\n        SeatMapper seatMapper,\n        GaInventoryRedisService gaInventoryRedisService,\n        GaInventoryClaimRepository gaInventoryClaimRepository,\n        StringRedisTemplate stringRedisTemplate\n    ) {\n        this.slotValidationService = slotValidationService;\n        this.seatRepository = seatRepository;\n        this.schedulingSlotClient = schedulingSlotClient;\n        this.seatMapper = seatMapper;\n        this.gaInventoryRedisService = gaInventoryRedisService;\n        this.gaInventoryClaimRepository = gaInventoryClaimRepository;\n        this.stringRedisTemplate = stringRedisTemplate;\n    }\n\n    public AvailableSeatsEnvelopeResponse getAvailability(Long slotId) {\n        SchedulingSlotResponse slot = slotValidationService.requireActiveAndSynced(slotId);\n        List<SchedulingPricingTierResponse> tiers = schedulingSlotClient.getPricingTiers(slotId);\n        Map<Long, SchedulingPricingTierResponse> tierMap = tiers.stream().collect(Collectors.toMap(SchedulingPricingTierResponse::id, Function.identity()));\n\n        if (slot.seatingMode() == SeatingMode.RESERVED) {\n            List<AvailableSeatResponse> seats = seatRepository.findAvailableForSlot(slotId, java.time.Instant.now())\n                .stream()\n                .map(seat -> {\n                    SchedulingPricingTierResponse tier = tierMap.get(seat.getPricingTierId());\n                    String tierName = tier == null ? null : tier.name();\n                    BigDecimal amount = tier == null ? BigDecimal.ZERO : tier.priceAmount();\n                    String currency = tier == null ? \"INR\" : tier.currency();\n                    return seatMapper.toAvailableSeatResponse(seat, tierName, amount, currency);\n                })\n                .toList();\n            return new AvailableSeatsEnvelopeResponse(slotId, slot.seatingMode(), seats, List.of());\n        }\n\n        List<GaTierAvailabilityResponse> gaTiers = tiers.stream()\n            .map(tier -> {\n                Long available = gaInventoryRedisService.getCurrent(slotId, tier.id());\n                if (available == null) {\n                    long confirmed = gaInventoryClaimRepository.countByShowSlotIdAndPricingTierIdAndLockState(slotId, tier.id(), SeatLockState.CONFIRMED);\n                    available = Math.max(0, tier.quota() - confirmed);\n                }\n                boolean blocked = Boolean.TRUE.equals(stringRedisTemplate.hasKey(\"tier:blocked:\" + slotId + \":\" + tier.id()));\n                return new GaTierAvailabilityResponse(\n                    tier.id(),\n                    tier.name(),\n                    tier.quota(),\n                    available,\n                    new Money(tier.priceAmount(), tier.currency()),\n                    blocked\n                );\n            })\n            .toList();\n\n        return new AvailableSeatsEnvelopeResponse(slotId, slot.seatingMode(), List.of(), gaTiers);\n    }\n}\npackage com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.arch;\n\nimport com.tngtech.archunit.junit.AnalyzeClasses;\nimport com.tngtech.archunit.junit.ArchTest;\nimport com.tngtech.archunit.lang.ArchRule;\n\nimport static com.tngtech.archunit.lang.syntax.ArchRuleDefinition.noClasses;\n\n@AnalyzeClasses(packages = \"com.eventplatform.bookinginventory\")\nclass BookingInventoryArchTest {\n\n    @ArchTest\n    static final ArchRule should_not_import_scheduling_domain_or_service =\n        noClasses()\n            .that().resideInAPackage(\"..bookinginventory..\")\n            .should().dependOnClassesThat()\n            .resideInAnyPackage(\n                \"..com.eventplatform.scheduling.domain..\",\n                \"..com.eventplatform.scheduling.service..\",\n                \"..com.eventplatform.scheduling.repository..\"\n            );\n}\n"
  }
}

CartPricingResult(zero, zero, zero, Map.of());\n        }\n\n        String currency = items.getFirst().getBasePrice().currency();\n        BigDecimal subtotalAmount = items.stream()\n            .map(this::lineAmount)\n            .reduce(BigDecimal.ZERO, BigDecimal::add);\n\n        Map<Long, List<CartItem>> byTier = tierId = entry.getKey();\n            List<CartItem> tierItems = entry.getValue();\n            GroupDiscountRule rule = rulesByTier.get(tierId);\n            if (rule == null || rule.getGroupDiscountThreshold() == null || rule.getGroupDiscountPercent() == null) {\n                tierItems.forEach(item -> itemDiscounts.put(item.getId(), new Money(BigDecimal.ZERO, tierItems.stream().map(this::lineAmount).reduce(BigDecimal.ZERO, BigDecimal::add);\n            BigDecimal tierDiscount = tierSubtotal\n                .multiply(rule.getGroupDiscountPercent())\n                .divide(new BigDecimal(\"100\"), 2, RoundingMode.HALF_UP);\n            totalDiscount = totalDiscount.add(tierDiscount);\n\n            for (CartItem item : tierItems) {\n                BigDecimal ratio = tierSubtotal.signum() == 0\n                    ? BigDecimal.ZERO\n                    : lineAmount(item).divide(tierSubtotal, 8, RoundingMode.HALF_UP);\n                BigDecimal itemDiscount = tierDiscount.multiply(ratio).setScale(2, RoundingMode.HALF_UP);\n                itemDiscounts.put(item.getId(), new Money(itemDiscount, currency));\n            }\n        }\n\n        Money subtotal = new Money(subtotalAmount.setScale(2, RoundingMode.HALF_UP), currency);\n        Money discount = new Money(totalDiscount.setScale(2, RoundingMode.HALF_UP), currency);\n        Money total = new Money(subtotal.amount().subtract(discount.amount()).setScale(2, RoundingMode.HALF_UP), currency);\n        cart.setDiscountAmount(discount);\n\n        return new CartPricingResult(subtotal, discount, total, itemDiscounts);\n    }\n\n    private BigDecimal lineAmount(CartItem item) {\n        return item.getBasePrice().amount().multiply(BigDecimal.valueOf(item.getQuantity()));\n    }\n}\npackage seats = seatRepository.findAvailableForSlot(slotId, java.time.Instant.now())\n                .stream()\n                .map(seat -> {\n                    SchedulingPricingTierResponse tier = tierMap.get(seat.getPricingTierId());\n                    String tierName = tier == null ? null : tier.name();\n                    BigDecimal amount = tier == null ? BigDecimal.ZERO : tier.priceAmount();\n                    String currency = tier == null ? \"INR\" : tier.currency();\n                    return seatMapper.toAvailableSeatResponse(seat, tierName, amount, currency);\n                })\n                .toList();\n            return new AvailableSeatsEnvelopeResponse(slotId, slot.seatingMode(), seats, List.of());\n        }\n\n        List<GaTierAvailabilityResponse> gaTiers = tiers.stream()\n            .map(tier -> {\n                Long available = gaInventoryRedisService.getCurrent(slotId, tier.id());\n                if (available == null) {\n                    long confirmed = gaInventoryClaimRepository.countByShowSlotIdAndPricingTierIdAndLockState(slotId, tier.id(), SeatLockState.CONFIRMED);\n                    available = Math.max(0, tier.quota() - confirmed);\n                }\n                boolean blocked = Boolean.TRUE.equals(stringRedisTemplate.hasKey(\"tier:blocked:\" + slotId + \":\" + tier.id()));\n                return new GaTierAvailabilityResponse(\n                    tier.id(),\n                    tier.name(),\n                    tier.quota(),\n                    available,\n                    new Money(tier.priceAmount(), tier.currency()),\n                    blocked\n                );\n            })\n            .toList();\n\n        return new AvailableSeatsEnvelopeResponse(slotId, slot.seatingMode(), List.of(), gaTiers);\n    }\n}\npackage com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.arch;\n\nimport com.tngtech.archunit.junit.AnalyzeClasses;\nimport com.tngtech.archunit.junit.ArchTest;\nimport com.tngtech.archunit.lang.ArchRule;\n\nimport static com.tngtech.archunit.lang.syntax.ArchRuleDefinition.noClasses;\n\n@AnalyzeClasses(packages = \"com.eventplatform.bookinginventory\")\nclass BookingInventoryArchTest {\n\n    @ArchTest\n    static final ArchRule should_not_import_scheduling_domain_or_service =\n        noClasses()\n            .that().resideInAPackage(\"..bookinginventory..\")\n            .should().dependOnClassesThat()\n            .resideInAnyPackage(\n                \"..com.eventplatform.scheduling.domain..\",\n                \"..com.eventplatform.scheduling.service..\",\n                \"..com.eventplatform.scheduling.repository..\"\n            );\n}\n"
  }
}
```

void markPendingSync() {\n        this.status = ShowSlotStatus.PENDING_SYNC;\n    }\n\n    public void markActive() {\n        this.status = ShowSlotStatus.ACTIVE;\n        this.syncAttemptCount = 0;\n        this.lastSyncError = null;\n        this.lastAttemptedAt = null;\n    }\n\n    public void markCancelled() {\n        this.status = ShowSlotStatus.CANCELLED;\n    }\n\n    public void     this.lastAttemptedAt = Instant.now();\n    }\n\n    public void setEbEventId(String ebEventId) {\n        this.ebEventId = ebEventId;\n    }\n\n    public void setEbSeriesId(String ebSeriesId) {\n jakarta.persistence.FetchType;\nimport jakarta.persistence.JoinColumn;\nimport jakarta.persistence.ManyToOne;\nimport jakarta.persistence.Table;\nimport java.math.BigDecimal;\n\n@Entity\n@Table(name = \"show_slot_pricing_tier\")\npublic class ShowSlotPricingTier extends BaseEntity {\n\n    @ManyToOne(fetch = FetchType.LAZY)\n    @JoinColumn(name = \"slot_id\", nullable = false)\n    private ShowSlot slot;\n\n    @Column(name = \"name\", nullable = false)\n    private String name;\n\n    @Embedded\n    @AttributeOverrides({\n        @AttributeOverride(name = \"amount\", column = @Column(name = \"price_amount\", nullable = false)),\n        @AttributeOverride(name = \"currency\", column = @Column(name = \"currency\", nullable = false))\n    })\n    private Money price;\n\n    @Column(name = \"quota\", nullable = false)\n    private Integer quota;\n\n    @Enumerated(EnumType.STRING)\n    @Column(name = \"tier_type\", nullable = false)\n    private TierType tierType;\n\n    @Column(name = \"eb_ticket_class_id\")\n    private String ebTicketClassId;\n\n    @Column(name = \"eb_inventory_tier_id\")\n    private String ebInventoryTierId;\n\n    @Column(name = \"group_discount_threshold\")\n    private Integer groupDiscountThreshold;   // nullable — null means no group discount\n\n    @Column(name = \"group_discount_percent\", precision = 5, scale = 2)\n    private BigDecimal groupDiscountPercent;  // nullable\n\n    protected ShowSlotPricingTier() {\n    }\n\n    public ShowSlotPricingTier(String name, Money price, Integer quota, TierType tierType) {\n        if (quota == null || quota <= 0) {\n            throw new BusinessRuleException(\"Pricing tier quota must be greater than 0\", \"INVALID_TIER_QUOTA\");\n        }\n        if (tierType == TierType.FREE && price != null && price.amount().signum() > 0) {\n            throw new BusinessRuleException(\"FREE tier must have priceAmount = 0\", \"INVALID_TIER_PRICE\");\n        }\n        this.name = name;\n        this.price = price;\n        this.quota = quota;\n        this.tierType = tierType;\n    }\n\n    public ShowSlot getSlot() {\n        return slot;\n    }\n\n    public String getName() {\n        return name;\n    }\n\n    public Money getPrice() {\n        return price;\n    }\n\n    public Integer getQuota() {\n        return quota;\n    }\n\n    public TierType getTierType() {\n        return tierType;\n    }\n\n    public String getEbTicketClassId() {\n        return ebTicketClassId;\n    }\n\n    public String getEbInventoryTierId() {\n        return ebInventoryTierId;\n    }\n\n    public Integer getGroupDiscountThreshold() {\n        return groupDiscountThreshold;\n    }\n\n    public BigDecimal getGroupDiscountPercent() {\n        return groupDiscountPercent;\n    }\n\n    public void setGroupDiscount(Integer threshold, BigDecimal percent) {\n        if (threshold != null && threshold <= 0) {\n            throw new BusinessRuleException(\"Group discount threshold must be > 0\", \"INVALID_GROUP_THRESHOLD\");\n        }\n        if (percent != null && (percent.compareTo(BigDecimal.ZERO) <= 0 || percent.compareTo(new BigDecimal(\"100\")) > 0)) {\n            throw new BusinessRuleException(\"Group discount percent must be between 0 and 100\", \"INVALID_GROUP_PERCENT\");\n        }\n        this.groupDiscountThreshold = threshold;\n        this.groupDiscountPercent   = percent;\n    }\n\n    public void attachTo(ShowSlot slot) {\n        this.slot = slot;\n    }\n\n    public void setEbTicketClassId(String ebTicketClassId) {\n        this.ebTicketClassId = ebTicketClassId;\n    }\n\n    public void setEbInventoryTierId(String ebInventoryTierId) {\n        this.ebInventoryTierId = ebInventoryTierId;\n    }\n}\n"
  }
}

groupDiscountPercent;\n    }\n\n    public void setGroupDiscount(Integer threshold, BigDecimal percent) {\n        if (threshold != null && threshold <= 0) {\n            throw new BusinessRuleException(\"Group discount threshold must be > 0\", \"INVALID_GROUP_THRESHOLD\");\n        }\n        if (percent != null && (percent.compareTo(BigDecimal.ZERO) <= 0 || percent.compareTo(new BigDecimal(\"100\")) > 0)) {\n            throw new BusinessRuleException(\"Group discount percent must be between 0 and 100\", \"INVALID_GROUP_PERCENT\");\n        }\n        this.groupDiscountThreshold = threshold;\n        this.groupDiscountPercent   = percent;\n    }\n\n    public void attachTo(ShowSlot slot) {\n        this.slot = slot;\n    }\n\n    public void setEbTicketClassId(String ebTicketClassId) {\n        this.ebTicketClassId = ebTicketClassId;\n    }\n\n    public void setEbInventoryTierId(String ebInventoryTierId) {\n        this.ebInventoryTierId = ebInventoryTierId;\n    }\n}\n"
  }
}
```

com.eventplatform.shared.common.event.published.TicketSyncCompletedEvent;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.common.event.published.TicketSyncFailedEvent;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.eventbrite.exception.EbIntegrationException;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.eventbrite.service.EbTicketService;\nimport java.math.BigDecimal;\nimport java.util.List;\nimport org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;\nimport org.junit.jupiter.api.extension.ExtendWith;\nimport org.mockito.InjectMocks;\nimport org.mockito.Mock;\nimport org.mockito.junit.jupiter.MockitoExtension;\nimport org.springframework.context.ApplicationEventPublisher;\n\n@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class)\nclass SlotTicketSyncServiceTest {\n\n    @Mock\n    private EbTicketService ebTicketService;\n    @Mock\n    private SchedulingSlotClient schedulingSlotClient;\n    @Mock\n    private ApplicationEventPublisher eventPublisher;\n\n    @InjectMocks\n    private SlotTicketSyncService slotTicketSyncService;\n\n    @Test\n    void should_call_createTicketClasses_and_inventoryTiers_when_syncing() {\n        when(schedulingSlotClient.getSlot(1L)).thenReturn(baseSlot(SeatingMode.GA, null));\n\n        slotTicketSyncService.syncTickets(new SlotDraftCreatedEvent(1L, \"eb-1\", List.of(1L), SeatingMode.GA, 1L, null));\n\n        verify(ebTicketService).createTicketClasses(eq(\"eb-1\"), any());\n        verify(ebTicketService).createInventoryTiers(eq(\"eb-1\"), any());\n        verify(eventPublisher).publishEvent(any(TicketSyncCompletedEvent.class));\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_call_copySeatMap_when_seatingMode_is_RESERVED() {\n        when(schedulingSlotClient.getSlot(1L)).thenReturn(baseSlot(SeatingMode.RESERVED, \"seatmap-1\"));\n\n        slotTicketSyncService.syncTickets(new SlotDraftCreatedEvent(1L, \"eb-1\", List.of(1L), SeatingMode.RESERVED, 1L, \"seatmap-1\"));\n\n        verify(ebTicketService).copySeatMap(\"eb-1\", \"seatmap-1\");\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_not_call_copySeatMap_when_seatingMode_is_GA() {\n        when(schedulingSlotClient.getSlot(1L)).thenReturn(baseSlot(SeatingMode.GA, null));\n\n        slotTicketSyncService.syncTickets(new SlotDraftCreatedEvent(1L, \"eb-1\", List.of(1L), SeatingMode.GA, 1L, null));\n\n        verify(ebTicketService, never()).copySeatMap(any(), any());\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_publish_TicketSyncFailedEvent_when_ticket_class_creation_fails() {\n        when(schedulingSlotClient.getSlot(1L)).thenReturn(baseSlot(SeatingMode.GA, null));\n        when(ebTicketService.createTicketClasses(eq(\"eb-1\"), any()))\n            .thenThrow(new EbIntegrationException(\"boom\"));\n\n        assertThatThrownBy(() -> slotTicketSyncService.syncTickets(\n            new SlotDraftCreatedEvent(1L, \"eb-1\", List.of(1L), SeatingMode.GA, 1L, null)))\n            .isInstanceOf(EbIntegrationException.class);\n\n        verify(eventPublisher).publishEvent(any(TicketSyncFailedEvent.class));\n    }\n\n    private SchedulingSlotResponse baseSlot(SeatingMode mode, String seatMapId) {\n        return new SchedulingSlotResponse(\n            1L,\n            10L,\n            20L,\n            \"eb-1\",\n            \"ACTIVE\",\n            mode,\n            seatMapId,\n            List.of(new SchedulingPricingTierResponse(1L, \"Free\", BigDecimal.ZERO, \"INR\", 10, \"FREE\", null, null, null))\n        );\n    }\n}\n"
  }
}

## Entry 518
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-04T13:15:22.158Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T13:15:22.158Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call",
    "name": "request_user_input",
    "arguments": "{\"questions\":[{\"header\":\"Client Removal\",\"id\":\"client_removal_scope\",\"question\":\"To satisfy your `SchedulingSlotClient` deletion constraint, should the refactor also migrate its non-hot-path callers (`SlotTicketSyncService`, `SlotValidationService`, `SeatProvisioningService`, and `EbInventoryValidationJob`) to the new shared readers?\", \"options\":[{\"label\":\"Migrate all callers (Recommended)\",\"description\":\"Removes `SchedulingSlotClient` completely and keeps architecture consistent everywhere.\"},{\"label\":\"Only hot-path services\",\"description\":\"Update only CartService/CartPricingService/SeatAvailabilityService and keep SchedulingSlotClient for other services.\"},{\"label\":\"Hybrid minimal\",\"description\":\"Migrate only callers needed to safely delete SchedulingSlotClient; leave unrelated classes unchanged.\"}]}]}",
    "call_id": "call_I6V37vOHpyxNADqikFigzzy0"
  }
}
```

## Entry 523
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-04T13:16:57.797Z
**Role/Type:** event_msg
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T13:16:57.797Z",
  "type": "event_msg",
  "payload": {
    "type": "item_completed",
    "thread_id": "019cb8cf-a0f1-74a3-9f91-8db984d07814",
    "turn_id": "019cb8fa-ccb0-77e1-9742-93b3c2fdadea",
    "item": {
      "type": "Plan",
      "id": "019cb8fa-ccb0-77e1-9742-93b3c2fdadea-plan",
      "text": "### Refactor Plan: Replace `booking-inventory -> scheduling` REST Reads with Shared Reader Interfaces\n\n#### Summary\nWe will remove hot-path (and remaining) HTTP reads from `booking-inventory` to `scheduling` by introducing two shared read interfaces in `shared/common/service` and implementing them inside `scheduling/service`.  \n`booking-inventory` will depend only on shared interfaces/DTOs, preserving module boundaries and hard rules.\n\nWe will also migrate all current `SchedulingSlotClient` callers so `SchedulingSlotClient.java` can be deleted safely (as requested).\n\n---\n\n### Scope and Decisions Locked\n\n- Baseline: **current working tree** (as-is).\n- `SchedulingSlotClient` strategy: **migrate all callers and delete it**.\n- Keep existing scheduling REST endpoints (`GET /api/v1/scheduling/slots/{slotId}`, `GET /api/v1/scheduling/slots/{slotId}/pricing-tiers`) intact for admin/external consumers.\n- DTO modeling:\n  - `SlotSummaryDto` includes all fields needed by booking-inventory callers.\n  - `PricingTierDto` uses `Money` (not raw price/currency primitives) per your constraint.\n- Reader impls are read-only services with `@Transactional(readOnly = true)`.\n\n---\n\n### Files to Add (New Contracts in `shared`)\n\n1. `shared/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/shared/common/service/SlotSummaryReader.java`\n- Interface method:\n  - `SlotSummaryDto getSlotSummary(Long slotId);`\n\n2. `shared/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/shared/common/service/SlotPricingReader.java`\n- Interface methods:\n  - `List<PricingTierDto> getSlotPricing(Long slotId);`\n  - (Optional wrapper not required; plain list is enough and simpler for cache path.)\n\n3. `shared/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/shared/common/dto/SlotSummaryDto.java`\n- Java record fields:\n  - `Long slotId`\n  - `String status`\n  - `String ebEventId`\n  - `SeatingMode seatingMode`\n  - `Long orgId`\n  - `Long venueId`\n  - `Long cityId`\n  - `String sourceSeatMapId`\n\n4. `shared/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/shared/common/dto/PricingTierDto.java`\n- Java record fields:\n  - `Long tierId`\n  - `String tierName`\n  - `Money price`\n  - `Integer quota`\n  - `String tierType`\n  - `String ebTicketClassId`\n  - `String ebInventoryTierId`\n  - `Integer groupDiscountThreshold`\n  - `BigDecimal groupDiscountPercent`\n\n---\n\n### Files to Add (Implementations in `scheduling`)\n\n5. `scheduling/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/scheduling/service/SlotSummaryReaderImpl.java`\n- `@Service`, implements `SlotSummaryReader`.\n- Inject `ShowSlotRepository`.\n- `@Transactional(readOnly = true)`.\n- Fetch slot by ID; if absent throw `ResourceNotFoundException(\"Slot not found: \" + slotId, \"SLOT_NOT_FOUND\")`.\n- Map from `ShowSlot` to `SlotSummaryDto`.\n\n6. `scheduling/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/scheduling/service/SlotPricingReaderImpl.java`\n- `@Service`, implements `SlotPricingReader`.\n- Inject `ShowSlotRepository`, `ShowSlotPricingTierRepository`.\n- `@Transactional(readOnly = true)`.\n- Guard slot existence via `ShowSlotRepository.existsById(slotId)` (or findById).\n- Fetch tiers using `findBySlotId(slotId)`.\n- Return `List.of()` when no tiers.\n- Map each tier to `PricingTierDto` (with `Money` carried from entity).\n\n---\n\n### Files to Update (booking-inventory service refactor)\n\n7. `booking-inventory/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/bookinginventory/service/CartService.java`\n- Replace `SchedulingSlotClient` dependency with:\n  - `SlotSummaryReader slotSummaryReader`\n  - `SlotPricingReader slotPricingReader` (if this class directly fetches tiers)\n- Replace slot reads:\n  - `slotValidationService.requireActiveAndSynced(slotId)` should internally use `SlotSummaryReader`.\n- Replace direct tier reads with `SlotPricingReader` (or delegate through updated `CartPricingService` where appropriate).\n- No business logic changes beyond data-source mechanism.\n\n8. `booking-inventory/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/bookinginventory/service/CartPricingService.java`\n- Inject:\n  - `SlotPricingReader slotPricingReader`\n  - `RedisTemplate<String, Object> redisTemplate`\n- Add cache-aside for pricing tiers:\n  - Key: `pricing:slot:{slotId}`\n  - On hit: return cached `List<PricingTierDto>`\n  - On miss: call `slotPricingReader.getSlotPricing(slotId)`, cache result with TTL 10 minutes, return result\n  - On Redis exceptions (read/write): log warn, fallback to direct reader call, no throw\n- Keep existing discount recompute logic unchanged.\n\n9. `booking-inventory/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/bookinginventory/service/SeatAvailabilityService.java`\n- Replace `SchedulingSlotClient` dependency with:\n  - `SlotSummaryReader`\n  - `CartPricingService` cached tier fetch helper (or `SlotPricingReader` directly; recommendation: use `CartPricingService` helper to centralize cache behavior).\n- No behavior changes beyond read source.\n\n10. Additional caller migrations required for deleting `SchedulingSlotClient`\n- `booking-inventory/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/bookinginventory/service/SlotValidationService.java`\n  - use `SlotSummaryReader`.\n- `booking-inventory/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/bookinginventory/service/SeatProvisioningService.java`\n  - use `SlotPricingReader`.\n- `booking-inventory/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/bookinginventory/service/SlotTicketSyncService.java`\n  - use `SlotSummaryReader + SlotPricingReader`.\n- `booking-inventory/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/bookinginventory/scheduler/EbInventoryValidationJob.java`\n  - remove unused `SchedulingSlotClient` dependency or swap to reader if needed.\n- Delete:\n  - `booking-inventory/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/bookinginventory/service/client/SchedulingSlotClient.java`\n  - `SchedulingSlotResponse.java`\n  - `SchedulingPricingTierResponse.java`\n  - plus test updates referencing these classes.\n\n---\n\n### ArchUnit Rule Update\n\n11. `booking-inventory/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/bookinginventory/arch/BookingInventoryArchTest.java`\n- Replace coarse rule with explicit package restrictions:\n  - forbid dependency on:\n    - `..com.eventplatform.scheduling.domain..`\n    - `..com.eventplatform.scheduling.repository..`\n    - `..com.eventplatform.scheduling.service..`\n- Add allowlist exceptions for shared contracts:\n  - `com.eventplatform.shared.common.service.SlotSummaryReader`\n  - `com.eventplatform.shared.common.service.SlotPricingReader`\n  - `com.eventplatform.shared.common.dto..`\n- Ensure test still fails if any direct scheduling internals are imported.\n\n---\n\n### Tests to Add/Update\n\n12. `scheduling/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/scheduling/service/SlotSummaryReaderImplTest.java`\n- Mockito unit test cases:\n  - returns DTO when slot exists\n  - throws `ResourceNotFoundException` when slot absent\n  - maps all required fields (`ebEventId`, `seatingMode`, etc.)\n\n13. `scheduling/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/scheduling/service/SlotPricingReaderImplTest.java`\n- Mockito unit test cases:\n  - returns mapped list when tiers exist\n  - returns empty list when no tiers\n  - maps `groupDiscountThreshold` and `groupDiscountPercent` correctly\n\n14. `booking-inventory/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/bookinginventory/service/CartPricingServiceTest.java`\n- Cache-aside tests:\n  - Redis hit -> reader not called\n  - Redis miss -> reader called and cache write attempted\n  - Redis read/write failure -> reader fallback works, no exception\n\n15. Update impacted existing tests:\n- `booking-inventory/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/bookinginventory/service/SlotTicketSyncServiceTest.java`\n  - replace `SchedulingSlotClient` mocks with `SlotSummaryReader`/`SlotPricingReader`\n  - keep behavioral assertions unchanged\n\n---\n\n### Implementation Notes (to keep code compilable)\n\n- Constructor injection only.\n- No new Maven dependencies.\n- Preserve existing scheduling controllers and response DTOs for API compatibility.\n- Keep `ResourceNotFoundException` from `shared/common/exception`.\n- `SeatingMode` remains enum from `shared/common/enums/SeatingMode`.\n- Monetary fields use `Money` in shared DTOs and mapping logic.\n\n---\n\n### Validation & Acceptance Criteria\n\n- Build compiles across modules.\n- No `booking-inventory` import of scheduling `domain/repository/service` packages.\n- `SchedulingSlotClient` removed and no remaining references.\n- Hot path reads (`available-seats`, `add-seat`, `confirm`) no longer perform internal HTTP to scheduling.\n- Reader tests + cart-pricing cache tests pass.\n- Existing scheduling REST endpoints still functional.\n\n---\n\n### Assumptions\n\n- `slotId` maps to `ShowSlot.id`.\n- `orgId` in current runtime is `ShowSlot.organizationId`.\n- `status` can remain string in `SlotSummaryDto` for minimal ripple (converted from `ShowSlotStatus.name()`).\n- Redis serializer can handle `List<PricingTierDto>` via existing `RedisTemplate<String,Object>` configuration.\n"
    }
  }
}
