{
  "payload": {
    "type": "item_completed",
    "thread_id": "019cb8cf-a0f1-74a3-9f91-8db984d07814",
    "turn_id": "019cb8fa-ccb0-77e1-9742-93b3c2fdadea",
    "item": {
      "type": "Plan",
      "id": "019cb8fa-ccb0-77e1-9742-93b3c2fdadea-plan",
      "text": "### Refactor Plan: Replace `booking-inventory -> scheduling` REST Reads with Shared Reader Interfaces\n\n#### Summary\nWe will remove hot-path (and remaining) HTTP reads from `booking-inventory` to `scheduling` by introducing two shared read interfaces in `shared/common/service` and implementing them inside `scheduling/service`.  \n`booking-inventory` will depend only on shared interfaces/DTOs, preserving module boundaries and hard rules.\n\nWe will also migrate all current `SchedulingSlotClient` callers so `SchedulingSlotClient.java` can be deleted safely (as requested).\n\n---\n\n### Scope and Decisions Locked\n\n- Baseline: **current working tree** (as-is).\n- `SchedulingSlotClient` strategy: **migrate all callers and delete it**.\n- Keep existing scheduling REST endpoints (`GET /api/v1/scheduling/slots/{slotId}`, `GET /api/v1/scheduling/slots/{slotId}/pricing-tiers`) intact for admin/external consumers.\n- DTO modeling:\n  - `SlotSummaryDto` includes all fields needed by booking-inventory callers.\n  - `PricingTierDto` uses `Money` (not raw price/currency primitives) per your constraint.\n- Reader impls are read-only services with `@Transactional(readOnly = true)`.\n\n---\n\n### Files to Add (New Contracts in `shared`)\n\n1. `shared/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/shared/common/service/SlotSummaryReader.java`\n- Interface method:\n  - `SlotSummaryDto getSlotSummary(Long slotId);`\n\n2. `shared/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/shared/common/service/SlotPricingReader.java`\n- Interface methods:\n  - `List<PricingTierDto> getSlotPricing(Long slotId);`\n  - (Optional wrapper not required; plain list is enough and simpler for cache path.)\n\n3. `shared/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/shared/common/dto/SlotSummaryDto.java`\n- Java record fields:\n  - `Long slotId`\n  - `String status`\n  - `String ebEventId`\n  - `SeatingMode seatingMode`\n  - `Long orgId`\n  - `Long venueId`\n  - `Long cityId`\n  - `String sourceSeatMapId`\n\n4. `shared/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/shared/common/dto/PricingTierDto.java`\n- Java record fields:\n  - `Long tierId`\n  - `String tierName`\n  - `Money price`\n  - `Integer quota`\n  - `String tierType`\n  - `String ebTicketClassId`\n  - `String ebInventoryTierId`\n  - `Integer groupDiscountThreshold`\n  - `BigDecimal groupDiscountPercent`\n\n---\n\n### Files to Add (Implementations in `scheduling`)\n\n5. `scheduling/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/scheduling/service/SlotSummaryReaderImpl.java`\n- `@Service`, implements `SlotSummaryReader`.\n- Inject `ShowSlotRepository`.\n- `@Transactional(readOnly = true)`.\n- Fetch slot by ID; if absent throw `ResourceNotFoundException(\"Slot not found: \" + slotId, \"SLOT_NOT_FOUND\")`.\n- Map from `ShowSlot` to `SlotSummaryDto`.\n\n6. `scheduling/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/scheduling/service/SlotPricingReaderImpl.java`\n- `@Service`, implements `SlotPricingReader`.\n- Inject `ShowSlotRepository`, `ShowSlotPricingTierRepository`.\n- `@Transactional(readOnly = true)`.\n- Guard slot existence via `ShowSlotRepository.existsById(slotId)` (or findById).\n- Fetch tiers using `findBySlotId(slotId)`.\n- Return `List.of()` when no tiers.\n- Map each tier to `PricingTierDto` (with `Money` carried from entity).\n\n---\n\n### Files to Update (booking-inventory service refactor)\n\n7. `booking-inventory/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/bookinginventory/service/CartService.java`\n- Replace `SchedulingSlotClient` dependency with:\n  - `SlotSummaryReader slotSummaryReader`\n  - `SlotPricingReader slotPricingReader` (if this class directly fetches tiers)\n- Replace slot reads:\n  - `slotValidationService.requireActiveAndSynced(slotId)` should internally use `SlotSummaryReader`.\n- Replace direct tier reads with `SlotPricingReader` (or delegate through updated `CartPricingService` where appropriate).\n- No business logic changes beyond data-source mechanism.\n\n8. `booking-inventory/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/bookinginventory/service/CartPricingService.java`\n- Inject:\n  - `SlotPricingReader slotPricingReader`\n  - `RedisTemplate<String, Object> redisTemplate`\n- Add cache-aside for pricing tiers:\n  - Key: `pricing:slot:{slotId}`\n  - On hit: return cached `List<PricingTierDto>`\n  - On miss: call `slotPricingReader.getSlotPricing(slotId)`, cache result with TTL 10 minutes, return result\n  - On Redis exceptions (read/write): log warn, fallback to direct reader call, no throw\n- Keep existing discount recompute logic unchanged.\n\n9. `booking-inventory/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/bookinginventory/service/SeatAvailabilityService.java`\n- Replace `SchedulingSlotClient` dependency with:\n  - `SlotSummaryReader`\n  - `CartPricingService` cached tier fetch helper (or `SlotPricingReader` directly; recommendation: use `CartPricingService` helper to centralize cache behavior).\n- No behavior changes beyond read source.\n\n10. Additional caller migrations required for deleting `SchedulingSlotClient`\n- `booking-inventory/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/bookinginventory/service/SlotValidationService.java`\n  - use `SlotSummaryReader`.\n- `booking-inventory/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/bookinginventory/service/SeatProvisioningService.java`\n  - use `SlotPricingReader`.\n- `booking-inventory/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/bookinginventory/service/SlotTicketSyncService.java`\n  - use `SlotSummaryReader + SlotPricingReader`.\n- `booking-inventory/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/bookinginventory/scheduler/EbInventoryValidationJob.java`\n  - remove unused `SchedulingSlotClient` dependency or swap to reader if needed.\n- Delete:\n  - `booking-inventory/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/bookinginventory/service/client/SchedulingSlotClient.java`\n  - `SchedulingSlotResponse.java`\n  - `SchedulingPricingTierResponse.java`\n  - plus test updates referencing these classes.\n\n---\n\n### ArchUnit Rule Update\n\n11. `booking-inventory/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/bookinginventory/arch/BookingInventoryArchTest.java`\n- Replace coarse rule with explicit package restrictions:\n  - forbid dependency on:\n    - `..com.eventplatform.scheduling.domain..`\n    - `..com.eventplatform.scheduling.repository..`\n    - `..com.eventplatform.scheduling.service..`\n- Add allowlist exceptions for shared contracts:\n  - `com.eventplatform.shared.common.service.SlotSummaryReader`\n  - `com.eventplatform.shared.common.service.SlotPricingReader`\n  - `com.eventplatform.shared.common.dto..`\n- Ensure test still fails if any direct scheduling internals are imported.\n\n---\n\n### Tests to Add/Update\n\n12. `scheduling/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/scheduling/service/SlotSummaryReaderImplTest.java`\n- Mockito unit test cases:\n  - returns DTO when slot exists\n  - throws `ResourceNotFoundException` when slot absent\n  - maps all required fields (`ebEventId`, `seatingMode`, etc.)\n\n13. `scheduling/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/scheduling/service/SlotPricingReaderImplTest.java`\n- Mockito unit test cases:\n  - returns mapped list when tiers exist\n  - returns empty list when no tiers\n  - maps `groupDiscountThreshold` and `groupDiscountPercent` correctly\n\n14. `booking-inventory/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/bookinginventory/service/CartPricingServiceTest.java`\n- Cache-aside tests:\n  - Redis hit -> reader not called\n  - Redis miss -> reader called and cache write attempted\n  - Redis read/write failure -> reader fallback works, no exception\n\n15. Update impacted existing tests:\n- `booking-inventory/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/bookinginventory/service/SlotTicketSyncServiceTest.java`\n  - replace `SchedulingSlotClient` mocks with `SlotSummaryReader`/`SlotPricingReader`\n  - keep behavioral assertions unchanged\n\n---\n\n### Implementation Notes (to keep code compilable)\n\n- Constructor injection only.\n- No new Maven dependencies.\n- Preserve existing scheduling controllers and response DTOs for API compatibility.\n- Keep `ResourceNotFoundException` from `shared/common/exception`.\n- `SeatingMode` remains enum from `shared/common/enums/SeatingMode`.\n- Monetary fields use `Money` in shared DTOs and mapping logic.\n\n---\n\n### Validation & Acceptance Criteria\n\n- Build compiles across modules.\n- No `booking-inventory` import of scheduling `domain/repository/service` packages.\n- `SchedulingSlotClient` removed and no remaining references.\n- Hot path reads (`available-seats`, `add-seat`, `confirm`) no longer perform internal HTTP to scheduling.\n- Reader tests + cart-pricing cache tests pass.\n- Existing scheduling REST endpoints still functional.\n\n---\n\n### Assumptions\n\n- `slotId` maps to `ShowSlot.id`.\n- `orgId` in current runtime is `ShowSlot.organizationId`.\n- `status` can remain string in `SlotSummaryDto` for minimal ripple (converted from `ShowSlotStatus.name()`).\n- Redis serializer can handle `List<PricingTierDto>` via existing `RedisTemplate<String,Object>` configuration.\n"
    }
  }
}

Additional caller migrations required for deleting `SchedulingSlotClient`\n- `booking-inventory/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/bookinginventory/service/SlotValidationService.java`\n  - use `SlotSummaryReader`.\n- `booking-inventory/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/bookinginventory/service/SeatProvisioningService.java`\n  - use `SlotPricingReader`.\n- `booking-inventory/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/bookinginventory/service/SlotTicketSyncService.java`\n  - use `SlotSummaryReader + SlotPricingReader`.\n- `booking-inventory/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/bookinginventory/scheduler/EbInventoryValidationJob.java`\n  - remove unused `SchedulingSlotClient` dependency or swap to reader if needed.\n- Delete:\n  - `booking-inventory/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/bookinginventory/service/client/SchedulingSlotClient.java`\n  - `SchedulingSlotResponse.java`\n  - `SchedulingPricingTierResponse.java`\n  - plus test updates referencing these classes.\n\n---\n\n### ArchUnit Rule Update\n\n11. `booking-inventory/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/bookinginventory/arch/BookingInventoryArchTest.java`\n- Replace coarse rule with explicit package restrictions:\n  - forbid dependency on:\n    - `..com.eventplatform.scheduling.domain..`\n    - `..com.eventplatform.scheduling.repository..`\n    - `..com.eventplatform.scheduling.service..`\n- Add allowlist exceptions for shared contracts:\n  - `com.eventplatform.shared.common.service.SlotSummaryReader`\n  - `com.eventplatform.shared.common.service.SlotPricingReader`\n  - `com.eventplatform.shared.common.dto..`\n- Ensure test still fails if any direct scheduling internals are imported.\n\n---\n\n### Tests to Add/Update\n\n12. `scheduling/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/scheduling/service/SlotSummaryReaderImplTest.java`\n- Mockito unit test cases:\n  - returns DTO when slot exists\n  - throws `ResourceNotFoundException` when slot absent\n  - maps all required fields (`ebEventId`, `seatingMode`, etc.)\n\n13. `scheduling/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/scheduling/service/SlotPricingReaderImplTest.java`\n- Mockito unit test cases:\n  - returns mapped list when tiers exist\n  - returns empty list when no tiers\n  - maps `groupDiscountThreshold` and `groupDiscountPercent` correctly\n\n14. `booking-inventory/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/bookinginventory/service/CartPricingServiceTest.java`\n- Cache-aside tests:\n  - Redis hit -> reader not called\n  - Redis miss -> reader called and cache write attempted\n  - Redis read/write failure -> reader fallback works, no exception\n\n15. Update impacted existing tests:\n- `booking-inventory/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/bookinginventory/service/SlotTicketSyncServiceTest.java`\n  - replace `SchedulingSlotClient` mocks with `SlotSummaryReader`/`SlotPricingReader`\n  - keep behavioral assertions unchanged\n\n---\n\n### Implementation Notes (to keep code compilable)\n\n- Constructor injection only.\n- No new Maven dependencies.\n- Preserve existing scheduling controllers and response DTOs for API compatibility.\n- Keep `ResourceNotFoundException` from `shared/common/exception`.\n- `SeatingMode` remains enum from `shared/common/enums/SeatingMode`.\n- Monetary fields use `Money` in shared DTOs and mapping logic.\n\n---\n\n### Validation & Acceptance Criteria\n\n- Build compiles across modules.\n- No `booking-inventory` import of scheduling `domain/repository/service` packages.\n- `SchedulingSlotClient` removed and no remaining references.\n- Hot path reads (`available-seats`, `add-seat`, `confirm`) no longer perform internal HTTP to scheduling.\n- Reader tests + cart-pricing cache tests pass.\n- Existing scheduling REST endpoints still functional.\n\n---\n\n### Assumptions\n\n- `slotId` maps to `ShowSlot.id`.\n- `orgId` in current runtime is `ShowSlot.organizationId`.\n- `status` can remain string in `SlotSummaryDto` for minimal ripple (converted from `ShowSlotStatus.name()`).\n- Redis serializer can handle `List<PricingTierDto>` via existing `RedisTemplate<String,Object>` configuration.\n</proposed_plan>"
      }
    ],
    "phase": "final_answer"
  }
}

## Entry 528
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-04T13:54:27.005Z
**Role/Type:** turn_context
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T13:54:27.005Z",
  "type": "turn_context",
  "payload": {
    "turn_id": "019cb920-e62f-7d51-baca-208ee92df7c2",
    "cwd": "/home/dell/Multi-City-Event-Booking",
    "current_date": "2026-03-04",
    "timezone": "Asia/Kolkata",
    "approval_policy": "on-request",
    "sandbox_policy": {
      "type": "workspace-write",
      "network_access": false,
      "exclude_tmpdir_env_var": false,
      "exclude_slash_tmp": false
    },
    "model": "gpt-5.3-codex",
    "personality": "pragmatic",
    "collaboration_mode": {
      "mode": "plan",
      "settings": {
        "model": "gpt-5.3-codex",
        "reasoning_effort": "medium",
        "developer_instructions": "# Plan Mode (Conversational)\n\nYou work in 3 phases, and you should *chat your way* to a great plan before finalizing it. A great plan is very detailed—intent- and implementation-wise—so that it can be handed to another engineer or agent to be implemented right away. It must be **decision complete**, where the implementer does not need to make any decisions.\n\n## Mode rules (strict)\n\nYou are in **Plan Mode** until a developer message explicitly ends it.\n\nPlan Mode is not changed by user intent, tone, or imperative language. If a user asks for execution while still in Plan Mode, treat it as a request to **plan the execution**, not perform it.\n\n## Plan Mode vs update_plan tool\n\nPlan Mode is a collaboration mode that can involve requesting user input and eventually issuing a `<proposed_plan>` block.\n\nSeparately, `update_plan` is a checklist/progress/TODOs tool; it does not enter or exit Plan Mode. Do not confuse it with Plan mode or try to use it while in Plan mode. If you try to use `update_plan` in Plan mode, it will return an error.\n\n## Execution vs. mutation in Plan Mode\n\nYou may explore and execute **non-mutating** actions that improve the plan. You must not perform **mutating** actions.\n\n### Allowed (non-mutating, plan-improving)\n\nActions that gather truth, reduce ambiguity, or validate feasibility without changing repo-tracked state. Examples:\n\n* Reading or searching files, configs, schemas, types, manifests, and docs\n* Static analysis, inspection, and repo exploration\n* Dry-run style commands when they do not edit repo-tracked files\n* Tests, builds, or checks that may write to caches or build artifacts (for example, `target/`, `.cache/`, or snapshots) so long as they do not edit repo-tracked files\n\n### Not allowed (mutating, plan-executing)\n\nActions that implement the plan or change repo-tracked state. Examples:\n\n* Editing or writing files\n* Running formatters or linters that rewrite files\n* Applying patches, migrations, or codegen that updates repo-tracked files\n* Side-effectful commands whose purpose is to carry out the plan rather than refine it\n\nWhen in doubt: if the action would reasonably be described as \"doing the work\" rather than \"planning the work,\" do not do it.\n\n## PHASE 1 — Ground in the environment (explore first, ask second)\n\nBegin by grounding yourself in the actual environment. Eliminate unknowns in the prompt by discovering facts, not by asking the user. Resolve all questions that can be answered through exploration or inspection. Identify missing or ambiguous details only if they cannot be derived from the environment. Silent exploration between turns is allowed and encouraged.\n\nBefore asking the user any question, perform at least one targeted non-mutating exploration pass (for example: search relevant files, inspect likely entrypoints/configs, confirm current implementation shape), unless no local environment/repo is available.\n\nException: you may ask clarifying questions about the user's prompt before exploring, ONLY if there are obvious ambiguities or contradictions in the prompt itself. However, if ambiguity might be resolved by exploring, always prefer exploring first.\n\nDo not ask questions that can be answered from the repo or system (for example, \"where is this struct?\" or \"which UI component should we use?\" when exploration can make it clear). Only ask once you have exhausted reasonable non-mutating exploration.\n\n## PHASE 2 — Intent chat (what they actually want)\n\n* Keep asking until you can clearly state: goal + success criteria, audience, in/out of scope, constraints, current state, and the key preferences/tradeoffs.\n* Bias toward questions over guessing: if any high-impact ambiguity remains, do NOT plan yet—ask.\n\n## PHASE 3 — Implementation chat (what/how we’ll build)\n\n* Once intent is stable, keep asking until the spec is decision complete: approach, interfaces (APIs/schemas/I/O), data flow, edge cases/failure modes, testing + acceptance criteria, rollout/monitoring, and any migrations/compat constraints.\n\n## Asking questions\n\nCritical rules:\n\n* Strongly prefer using the `request_user_input` tool to ask any questions.\n* Offer only meaningful multiple‑choice options; don’t include filler choices that are obviously wrong or irrelevant.\n* In rare cases where an unavoidable, important question can’t be expressed with reasonable multiple‑choice options (due to extreme ambiguity), you may ask it directly without the tool.\n\nYou SHOULD ask many questions, but each question must:\n\n* materially change the spec/plan, OR\n* confirm/lock an assumption, OR\n* choose between meaningful tradeoffs.\n* not be answerable by non-mutating commands.\n\nUse the `request_user_input` tool only for decisions that materially change the plan, for confirming important assumptions, or for information that cannot be discovered via non-mutating exploration.\n\n## Two kinds of unknowns (treat differently)\n\n1. **Discoverable facts** (repo/system truth): explore first.\n\n   * Before asking, run targeted searches and check likely sources of truth (configs/manifests/entrypoints/schemas/types/constants).\n   * Ask only if: multiple plausible candidates; nothing found but you need a missing identifier/context; or ambiguity is actually product intent.\n   * If asking, present concrete candidates (paths/service names) + recommend one.\n   * Never ask questions you can answer from your environment (e.g., “where is this struct”).\n\n2. **Preferences/tradeoffs** (not discoverable): ask early.\n\n   * These are intent or implementation preferences that cannot be derived from exploration.\n   * Provide 2–4 mutually exclusive options + a recommended default.\n   * If unanswered, proceed with the recommended option and record it as an assumption in the final plan.\n\n## Finalization rule\n\nOnly output the final plan when it is decision complete and leaves no decisions to the implementer.\n\nWhen you present the official plan, wrap it in a `<proposed_plan>` block so the client can render it specially:\n\n1) The opening tag must be on its own line.\n2) Start the plan content on the next line (no text on the same line as the tag).\n3) The closing tag must be on its own line.\n4) Use Markdown inside the block.\n5) Keep the tags exactly as `<proposed_plan>` and `</proposed_plan>` (do not translate or rename them), even if the plan content is in another language.\n\nExample:\n\n<proposed_plan>\nplan content\n</proposed_plan>\n\nplan content should be human and agent digestible. The final plan must be plan-only and include:\n\n* A clear title\n* A brief summary section\n* Important changes or additions to public APIs/interfaces/types\n* Test cases and scenarios\n* Explicit assumptions and defaults chosen where needed\n\nDo not ask \"should I proceed?\" in the final output. The user can easily switch out of Plan mode and request implementation if you have included a `<proposed_plan>` block in your response. Alternatively, they can decide to stay in Plan mode and continue refining the plan.\n\nOnly produce at most one `<proposed_plan>` block per turn, and only when you are presenting a complete spec.\n\nIf the user stays in Plan mode and asks for revisions after a prior `<proposed_plan>`, any new `<proposed_plan>` must be a complete replacement.\n"
      }
    },
    "effort": "medium",
    "summary": "none",
    "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md — Reference Index\n\n> Every agent working in this codebase must read the files listed below before producing\n> any output. Each file governs a specific concern. Read the ones that apply to your task.\n\n---\n\n## Reference Files\n\n| File | Governs |\n|---|---|\n| `PRODUCT.md` | The 7 modules, what each one owns, dependency order, inter-module event map, Eventbrite ACL facade names, hard architectural rules, and where new code goes. Read this before touching any module. |\n| `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` | Eventbrite integration architecture: ACL facades, all FR flows (planned vs. implemented), critical constraints (no user/order creation, no single-order cancel, no seat locking), workarounds. Read before designing any Eventbrite feature. |\n| `docs/MODULE_STRUCTURE_TEMPLATE.md` | Canonical folder and package layout for every module. The exact location of every layer: `api/controller/`, `api/dto/`, `domain/`, `service/`, `repository/`, `mapper/`, `event/published/`, `event/listener/`, `statemachine/`, `exception/`. Anti-patterns list. Read this before writing any class. |\n| `agents/SPEC_GENERATOR.md` | How to produce a `SPEC.md` for any feature. Eight confirmation-gated stages with module context and Eventbrite constraints built-in. Requirements → Domain Model → Architecture & File Structure → DB Schema → API → Business Logic → Error Handling → Tests. Read this before speccing any feature. |\n| `agents/CODING_AGENT.md` | Spring Boot 3.5.11 / Java 21 coding standards. Domain behaviour, service orchestration, mapper rules, controller rules, event rules, 10 Eventbrite ACL facades, inter-module communication (REST + Spring Events), admin/ special rules, state machine, Spring AI. Read this before writing any production code. |\n| `agents/TEST_AGENT.md` | Test agent responsibilities and best practices. Builds tests from `SPEC_*.md` Stage 8, executes tests, fixes test/code issues caused by behavior mismatches, and writes timestamped logs under `test_result_folder/`. Read this before writing or running tests. |\n| `docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md` | Four-layer test structure: `domain/` (unit), `service/` (Mockito), `api/` (@WebMvcTest), `arch/` (ArchUnit enforcing 10 hard rules + Eventbrite constraints). All rule examples, happy path, negative path, exception patterns. Read before writing any test. |\n| `mock-eventbrite-api/README.md` | **Mock Eventbrite Service:** Python FastAPI in-memory mock of Eventbrite API v3. Used for local development and testing. Exposes all 10 ACL facade endpoints without hitting production Eventbrite. See setup instructions below. |\n\n---\n\n## Module Quick Reference\n\nFull detail for each module is in `PRODUCT.md`. This table is a lookup only.\n\n| Module | Owns | Eventbrite ACL Facades Used |\n|---|---|---|\n| `discovery-catalog` | Events, Venues, Cities, catalog sync | `EbEventSyncService`, `EbTicketService` |\n| `scheduling` | Show slots, conflict validation | `EbEventSyncService` |\n| `identity` | Users, JWT, profiles, preferences, wallet | `EbAttendeeService` |\n| `booking-inventory` | Seats, SeatMap, Cart, Pricing, Seat Lock State Machine | `EbTicketService` |\n| `payments-ticketing` | Bookings, Payments, E-Tickets, Cancellations, Refunds, Wallet | `EbOrderService`, `EbAttendeeService`, `EbRefundService` |\n| `promotions` | Coupons, Promotions, Eligibility rules | `EbDiscountSyncService` |\n| `engagement` | Reviews, Moderation, AI Chatbot (`service/chatbot/`), RAG pipeline | `EbEventSyncService` |\n| `admin/` | No domain ownership. Orchestration and aggregation only. | — |\n| `shared/` | All external ACL facades, JWT, base classes, common DTOs, configs | — |\n\n---\n\n## Hard Rules — Never Violate\n\nCanonical numbering and wording are owned by `PRODUCT.md` Section 9. These shorthand rules apply to every task without exception.\n\n1. No module imports another module's `@Service`, `@Entity`, or `@Repository`.\n  - Exception: `admin/` MAY import module `@Service` beans for READ operations only; all writes MUST go through the owning module REST API.\n2. No module calls Eventbrite, Razorpay, or OpenAI HTTP directly — only through `shared/` ACL facades.\n3. ACL facade names are exactly as listed below — never invent new ones.\n4. Spring Events carry only primitives, IDs, enums, and value objects — never `@Entity` objects.\n5. One `GlobalExceptionHandler` in `shared/common/exception/` — never `@ControllerAdvice` in a module.\n6. All field mapping goes in `mapper/` using MapStruct — never inside a service or controller.\n7. `admin/` owns no `@Entity` and no database table.\n8. `shared/` has no dependency on any module.\n9. `app/` has zero business logic.\n10. Dependency direction is strictly downward — no circular imports between modules.\n\n---\n\n## Eventbrite ACL Facade Names (HARD RULE #3)\nRead the docs/eventbriteapiv3public.apib for detail API Endpoint with schema, Response, Request and errors\n\nAll 10 facades are in `shared/eventbrite/service/`. These are the ONLY things modules call for Eventbrite:\n\n1. **EbEventSyncService** — Create, update, publish, cancel events; pull org event catalog\n2. **EbVenueService** — Create and update venues; list org venues\n3. **EbScheduleService** — Create recurring event schedules and occurrences\n4. **EbTicketService** — Create/update ticket classes; manage inventory; check availability\n5. **EbCapacityService** — Retrieve and update event capacity tiers\n6. **EbOrderService** — Read orders post-checkout (NOT creation — JS SDK widget only)\n7. **EbAttendeeService** — List attendees; verify attendance for reviews\n8. **EbDiscountSyncService** — Create, update, delete discount codes\n9. **EbRefundService** — Read refund status only (NO submission API — workaround required)\n10. **EbWebhookService** — Mock/testing only; not used in production registration flow\n\n**See `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` for which module uses which facade and what gaps exist.**\n\n---\n\n## Module Dependency Order (HARD RULE #10)\n\n```\nshared/               <- foundational; no dependencies\n  |\n  v\ndiscovery-catalog    <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nscheduling           <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\nidentity             <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nbooking-inventory    <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\n  |\n  v\npayments-ticketing   <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\npromotions           <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nengagement           <- depends on shared + payments-ticketing + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nadmin/               <- thin orchestration; reads from all, writes via REST APIs\n  |\n  v\napp/                 <- entry point only; zero business logic\n```\n\n**Golden Rule:** Module at level N can depend on level N-1 or lower (or `shared/`) only. Never import from sibling or downstream modules.\n\n**See `PRODUCT.md` Section 2 for detailed rationale.**\n\n\n## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \"how do I do X\", \"find a skill for X\", \"is there a skill that can...\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. (file: /home/dell/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md)\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.",
    "truncation_policy": {
      "mode": "tokens",
      "limit": 10000
    }
  }
}
```

        "developer_instructions": "# Plan Mode (Conversational)\n\nYou work in 3 phases, and you should *chat your way* to a great plan before finalizing it. A great plan is very detailed—intent- and implementation-wise—so that it can be handed to another engineer or agent to be implemented right away. It must be **decision complete**, where the implementer does not need to make any decisions.\n\n## Mode rules (strict)\n\nYou are in **Plan Mode** until a developer message explicitly ends it.\n\nPlan Mode is not changed by user intent, tone, or imperative language. If a user asks for execution while still in Plan Mode, treat it as a request to **plan the execution**, not perform it.\n\n## Plan Mode vs update_plan tool\n\nPlan Mode is a collaboration mode that can involve requesting user input and eventually issuing a `<proposed_plan>` block.\n\nSeparately, `update_plan` is a checklist/progress/TODOs tool; it does not enter or exit Plan Mode. Do not confuse it with Plan mode or try to use it while in Plan mode. If you try to use `update_plan` in Plan mode, it will return an error.\n\n## Execution vs. mutation in Plan Mode\n\nYou may explore and execute **non-mutating** actions that improve the plan. You must not perform **mutating** actions.\n\n### Allowed (non-mutating, plan-improving)\n\nActions that gather truth, reduce ambiguity, or validate feasibility without changing repo-tracked state. Examples:\n\n* Reading or searching files, configs, schemas, types, manifests, and docs\n* Static analysis, inspection, and repo exploration\n* Dry-run style commands when they do not edit repo-tracked files\n* Tests, builds, or checks that may write to caches or build artifacts (for example, `target/`, `.cache/`, or snapshots) so long as they do not edit repo-tracked files\n\n### Not allowed (mutating, plan-executing)\n\nActions that implement the plan or change repo-tracked state. Examples:\n\n* Editing or writing files\n* Running formatters or linters that rewrite files\n* Applying patches, migrations, or codegen that updates repo-tracked files\n* Side-effectful commands whose purpose is to carry out the plan rather than refine it\n\nWhen in doubt: if the action would reasonably be described as \"doing the work\" rather than \"planning the work,\" do not do it.\n\n## PHASE 1 — Ground in the environment (explore first, ask second)\n\nBegin by grounding yourself in the actual environment. Eliminate unknowns in the prompt by discovering facts, not by asking the user. Resolve all questions that can be answered through exploration or inspection. Identify missing or ambiguous details only if they cannot be derived from the environment. Silent exploration between turns is allowed and encouraged.\n\nBefore asking the user any question, perform at least one targeted non-mutating exploration pass (for example: search relevant files, inspect likely entrypoints/configs, confirm current implementation shape), unless no local environment/repo is available.\n\nException: you may ask clarifying questions about the user's prompt before exploring, ONLY if there are obvious ambiguities or contradictions in the prompt itself. However, if ambiguity might be resolved by exploring, always prefer exploring first.\n\nDo not ask questions that can be answered from the repo or system (for example, \"where is this struct?\" or \"which UI component should we use?\" when exploration can make it clear). Only ask once you have exhausted reasonable non-mutating exploration.\n\n## PHASE 2 — Intent chat (what they actually want)\n\n* Keep asking until you can clearly state: goal + success criteria, audience, in/out of scope, constraints, current state, and the key preferences/tradeoffs.\n* Bias toward questions over guessing: if any high-impact ambiguity remains, do NOT plan yet—ask.\n\n## PHASE 3 — Implementation chat (what/how we’ll build)\n\n* Once intent is stable, keep asking until the spec is decision complete: approach, interfaces (APIs/schemas/I/O), data flow, edge cases/failure modes, testing + acceptance criteria, rollout/monitoring, and any migrations/compat constraints.\n\n## Asking questions\n\nCritical rules:\n\n* Strongly prefer using the `request_user_input` tool to ask any questions.\n* Offer only meaningful multiple‑choice options; don’t include filler choices that are obviously wrong or irrelevant.\n* In rare cases where an unavoidable, important question can’t be expressed with reasonable multiple‑choice options (due to extreme ambiguity), you may ask it directly without the tool.\n\nYou SHOULD ask many questions, but each question must:\n\n* materially change the spec/plan, OR\n* confirm/lock an assumption, OR\n* choose between meaningful tradeoffs.\n* not be answerable by non-mutating commands.\n\nUse the `request_user_input` tool only for decisions that materially change the plan, for confirming important assumptions, or for information that cannot be discovered via non-mutating exploration.\n\n## Two kinds of unknowns (treat differently)\n\n1. **Discoverable facts** (repo/system truth): explore first.\n\n   * Before asking, run targeted searches and check likely sources of truth (configs/manifests/entrypoints/schemas/types/constants).\n   * Ask only if: multiple plausible candidates; nothing found but you need a missing identifier/context; or ambiguity is actually product intent.\n   * If asking, present concrete candidates (paths/service names) + recommend one.\n   * Never ask questions you can answer from your environment (e.g., “where is this struct”).\n\n2. **Preferences/tradeoffs** (not discoverable): ask early.\n\n   * These are intent or implementation preferences that cannot be derived from exploration.\n   * Provide 2–4 mutually exclusive options + a recommended default.\n   * If unanswered, proceed with the recommended option and record it as an assumption in the final plan.\n\n## Finalization rule\n\nOnly output the final plan when it is decision complete and leaves no decisions to the implementer.\n\nWhen you present the official plan, wrap it in a `<proposed_plan>` block so the client can render it specially:\n\n1) The opening tag must be on its own line.\n2) Start the plan content on the next line (no text on the same line as the tag).\n3) The closing tag must be on its own line.\n4) Use Markdown inside the block.\n5) Keep the tags exactly as `<proposed_plan>` and `</proposed_plan>` (do not translate or rename them), even if the plan content is in another language.\n\nExample:\n\n<proposed_plan>\nplan content\n</proposed_plan>\n\nplan content should be human and agent digestible. The final plan must be plan-only and include:\n\n* A clear title\n* A brief summary section\n* Important changes or additions to public APIs/interfaces/types\n* Test cases and scenarios\n* Explicit assumptions and defaults chosen where needed\n\nDo not ask \"should I proceed?\" in the final output. The user can easily switch out of Plan mode and request implementation if you have included a `<proposed_plan>` block in your response. Alternatively, they can decide to stay in Plan mode and continue refining the plan.\n\nOnly produce at most one `<proposed_plan>` block per turn, and only when you are presenting a complete spec.\n\nIf the user stays in Plan mode and asks for revisions after a prior `<proposed_plan>`, any new `<proposed_plan>` must be a complete replacement.\n"

    "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md — Reference Index\n\n> Every agent working in this codebase must read the files listed below before producing\n> any output. Each file governs a specific concern. Read the ones that apply to your task.\n\n---\n\n## Reference Files\n\n| File | Governs |\n|---|---|\n| `PRODUCT.md` | The 7 modules, what each one owns, dependency order, inter-module event map, Eventbrite ACL facade names, hard architectural rules, and where new code goes. Read this before touching any module. |\n| `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` | Eventbrite integration architecture: ACL facades, all FR flows (planned vs. implemented), critical constraints (no user/order creation, no single-order cancel, no seat locking), workarounds. Read before designing any Eventbrite feature. |\n| `docs/MODULE_STRUCTURE_TEMPLATE.md` | Canonical folder and package layout for every module. The exact location of every layer: `api/controller/`, `api/dto/`, `domain/`, `service/`, `repository/`, `mapper/`, `event/published/`, `event/listener/`, `statemachine/`, `exception/`. Anti-patterns list. Read this before writing any class. |\n| `agents/SPEC_GENERATOR.md` | How to produce a `SPEC.md` for any feature. Eight confirmation-gated stages with module context and Eventbrite constraints built-in. Requirements → Domain Model → Architecture & File Structure → DB Schema → API → Business Logic → Error Handling → Tests. Read this before speccing any feature. |\n| `agents/CODING_AGENT.md` | Spring Boot 3.5.11 / Java 21 coding standards. Domain behaviour, service orchestration, mapper rules, controller rules, event rules, 10 Eventbrite ACL facades, inter-module communication (REST + Spring Events), admin/ special rules, state machine, Spring AI. Read this before writing any production code. |\n| `agents/TEST_AGENT.md` | Test agent responsibilities and best practices. Builds tests from `SPEC_*.md` Stage 8, executes tests, fixes test/code issues caused by behavior mismatches, and writes timestamped logs under `test_result_folder/`. Read this before writing or running tests. |\n| `docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md` | Four-layer test structure: `domain/` (unit), `service/` (Mockito), `api/` (@WebMvcTest), `arch/` (ArchUnit enforcing 10 hard rules + Eventbrite constraints). All rule examples, happy path, negative path, exception patterns. Read before writing any test. |\n| `mock-eventbrite-api/README.md` | **Mock Eventbrite Service:** Python FastAPI in-memory mock of Eventbrite API v3. Used for local development and testing. Exposes all 10 ACL facade endpoints without hitting production Eventbrite. See setup instructions below. |\n\n---\n\n## Module Quick Reference\n\nFull detail for each module is in `PRODUCT.md`. This table is a lookup only.\n\n| Module | Owns | Eventbrite ACL Facades Used |\n|---|---|---|\n| `discovery-catalog` | Events, Venues, Cities, catalog sync | `EbEventSyncService`, `EbTicketService` |\n| `scheduling` | Show slots, conflict validation | `EbEventSyncService` |\n| `identity` | Users, JWT, profiles, preferences, wallet | `EbAttendeeService` |\n| `booking-inventory` | Seats, SeatMap, Cart, Pricing, Seat Lock State Machine | `EbTicketService` |\n| `payments-ticketing` | Bookings, Payments, E-Tickets, Cancellations, Refunds, Wallet | `EbOrderService`, `EbAttendeeService`, `EbRefundService` |\n| `promotions` | Coupons, Promotions, Eligibility rules | `EbDiscountSyncService` |\n| `engagement` | Reviews, Moderation, AI Chatbot (`service/chatbot/`), RAG pipeline | `EbEventSyncService` |\n| `admin/` | No domain ownership. Orchestration and aggregation only. | — |\n| `shared/` | All external ACL facades, JWT, base classes, common DTOs, configs | — |\n\n---\n\n## Hard Rules — Never Violate\n\nCanonical numbering and wording are owned by `PRODUCT.md` Section 9. These shorthand rules apply to every task without exception.\n\n1. No module imports another module's `@Service`, `@Entity`, or `@Repository`.\n  - Exception: `admin/` MAY import module `@Service` beans for READ operations only; all writes MUST go through the owning module REST API.\n2. No module calls Eventbrite, Razorpay, or OpenAI HTTP directly — only through `shared/` ACL facades.\n3. ACL facade names are exactly as listed below — never invent new ones.\n4. Spring Events carry only primitives, IDs, enums, and value objects — never `@Entity` objects.\n5. One `GlobalExceptionHandler` in `shared/common/exception/` — never `@ControllerAdvice` in a module.\n6. All field mapping goes in `mapper/` using MapStruct — never inside a service or controller.\n7. `admin/` owns no `@Entity` and no database table.\n8. `shared/` has no dependency on any module.\n9. `app/` has zero business logic.\n10. Dependency direction is strictly downward — no circular imports between modules.\n\n---\n\n## Eventbrite ACL Facade Names (HARD RULE #3)\nRead the docs/eventbriteapiv3public.apib for detail API Endpoint with schema, Response, Request and errors\n\nAll 10 facades are in `shared/eventbrite/service/`. These are the ONLY things modules call for Eventbrite:\n\n1. **EbEventSyncService** — Create, update, publish, cancel events; pull org event catalog\n2. **EbVenueService** — Create and update venues; list org venues\n3. **EbScheduleService** — Create recurring event schedules and occurrences\n4. **EbTicketService** — Create/update ticket classes; manage inventory; check availability\n5. **EbCapacityService** — Retrieve and update event capacity tiers\n6. **EbOrderService** — Read orders post-checkout (NOT creation — JS SDK widget only)\n7. **EbAttendeeService** — List attendees; verify attendance for reviews\n8. **EbDiscountSyncService** — Create, update, delete discount codes\n9. **EbRefundService** — Read refund status only (NO submission API — workaround required)\n10. **EbWebhookService** — Mock/testing only; not used in production registration flow\n\n**See `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` for which module uses which facade and what gaps exist.**\n\n---\n\n## Module Dependency Order (HARD RULE #10)\n\n```\nshared/               <- foundational; no dependencies\n  |\n  v\ndiscovery-catalog    <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nscheduling           <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\nidentity             <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nbooking-inventory    <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\n  |\n  v\npayments-ticketing   <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\npromotions           <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nengagement           <- depends on shared + payments-ticketing + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nadmin/               <- thin orchestration; reads from all, writes via REST APIs\n  |\n  v\napp/                 <- entry point only; zero business logic\n```\n\n**Golden Rule:** Module at level N can depend on level N-1 or lower (or `shared/`) only. Never import from sibling or downstream modules.\n\n**See `PRODUCT.md` Section 2 for detailed rationale.**\n\n\n## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \"how do I do X\", \"find a skill for X\", \"is there a skill that can...\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. (file: /home/dell/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md)\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.",

verifies `tierId`, `tierName`, `price` (Money), `groupDiscountThreshold`, `groupDiscountPercent`.\n- Returns `List.of()` when `findBySlotId` returns empty list.\n- Throws `ResourceNotFoundException` when `existsById` returns false.\n\n**17. `booking-inventory/src/test/java/.../service/CartPricingServiceTest.java`** (new)\n\nCache-aside tests for `getSlotPricingCached()`:\n- **Redis hit**: `stringRedisTemplate.opsForValue().get(key)` returns valid JSON → `slotPricingReader.getSlotPricing()` is **never** called.\n- **Redis miss**: `get()` returns `null` → `slotPricingReader.getSlotPricing()` is called once → `set()` is called with serialized JSON and 10-minute TTL.\n- **Redis read failure**: `get()` throws `RedisConnectionFailureException` → fallback calls `slotPricingReader.getSlotPricing()`, no exception propagated.\n- **Redis write failure**: `get()` returns null (miss), reader called, `set()` throws → no exception propagated, result still returned.\n- **JSON deserialization failure** (corrupt cache): `get()` returns garbage string → fallback to reader, no exception propagated.\n\n**18. SlotTicketSyncServiceTest.java** (update)\n\n- Remove `@Mock SchedulingSlotClient schedulingSlotClient` and all related imports.\n- Add `@Mock SlotSummaryReader slotSummaryReader` and `@Mock SlotPricingReader slotPricingReader`.\n- Update `@InjectMocks SlotTicketSyncService` constructor — it now takes `(EbTicketService, SlotSummaryReader, SlotPricingReader, ApplicationEventPublisher)`.\n- Split the existing `baseSlot()` helper into two:\n  - `private SlotSummaryDto baseSummary(SeatingMode mode, String seatMapId)` — returns a `SlotSummaryDto`.\n  - `private List<PricingTierDto> baseTiers()` — returns `List.of(new PricingTierDto(1L, \"Free\", new Money(BigDecimal.ZERO, \"INR\"), 10, \"FREE\", null, null, null, null))`.\n- Update all 4 test stubs: `when(schedulingSlotClient.getSlot(1L)).thenReturn(...)` → `when(slotSummaryReader.getSlotSummary(1L)).thenReturn(baseSummary(...))` plus `when(slotPricingReader.getSlotPricing(1L)).thenReturn(baseTiers())`.\n- All behavioral `verify(...)` assertions remain unchanged.\n\n---\n\n### Implementation Notes\n\n- Constructor injection only.\n- No new Maven dependencies (Jackson is already on the classpath; `StringRedisTemplate` is already used throughout the module).\n- `SlotPricingReader` and `SlotSummaryReader` interfaces in shared must be in the shared module's pom-declared dependencies for booking-inventory (already satisfied since shared is on the classpath).\n- `Money` is already a shared record — no move needed.\n- `SeatingMode` remains enum from `shared/common/enums/`.\n- Preserve all existing scheduling controllers, response DTOs, and REST endpoints for admin consumers.\n- `ResourceNotFoundException` is in `shared/common/exception/` — confirmed present.\n\n---\n\n### Validation & Acceptance Criteria\n\n- Build compiles across all modules with zero errors.\n- No booking-inventory class imports from `scheduling.domain`, `scheduling.service`, or `scheduling.repository` (ArchUnit enforces this unchanged).\n- `SchedulingSlotClient`, `SchedulingSlotResponse`, `SchedulingPricingTierResponse` are deleted with zero remaining references anywhere.\n- `GaInventoryService.initCounters()` accepts `List<PricingTierDto>` — no `SchedulingPricingTierResponse` references remain in any booking-inventory production class.\n- Hot-path reads (`getAvailability`, `addSeat`, `confirm`) no longer perform internal HTTP calls.\n- `SlotSummaryReaderImplTest`, `SlotPricingReaderImplTest`, `CartPricingServiceTest` all pass.\n- `SlotTicketSyncServiceTest` passes with updated mocks and unchanged behavioral assertions.\n- Existing scheduling REST endpoints remain functional (`GET /api/v1/scheduling/slots/{id}`, `GET /api/v1/scheduling/slots/{id}/pricing-tiers`). here is the u[dated plan"

verifies `tierId`, `tierName`, `price` (Money), `groupDiscountThreshold`, `groupDiscountPercent`.\n- Returns `List.of()` when `findBySlotId` returns empty list.\n- Throws `ResourceNotFoundException` when `existsById` returns false.\n\n**17. `booking-inventory/src/test/java/.../service/CartPricingServiceTest.java`** (new)\n\nCache-aside tests for `getSlotPricingCached()`:\n- **Redis hit**: `stringRedisTemplate.opsForValue().get(key)` returns valid JSON → `slotPricingReader.getSlotPricing()` is **never** called.\n- **Redis miss**: `get()` returns `null` → `slotPricingReader.getSlotPricing()` is called once → `set()` is called with serialized JSON and 10-minute TTL.\n- **Redis read failure**: `get()` throws `RedisConnectionFailureException` → fallback calls `slotPricingReader.getSlotPricing()`, no exception propagated.\n- **Redis write failure**: `get()` returns null (miss), reader called, `set()` throws → no exception propagated, result still returned.\n- **JSON deserialization failure** (corrupt cache): `get()` returns garbage string → fallback to reader, no exception propagated.\n\n**18. SlotTicketSyncServiceTest.java** (update)\n\n- Remove `@Mock SchedulingSlotClient schedulingSlotClient` and all related imports.\n- Add `@Mock SlotSummaryReader slotSummaryReader` and `@Mock SlotPricingReader slotPricingReader`.\n- Update `@InjectMocks SlotTicketSyncService` constructor — it now takes `(EbTicketService, SlotSummaryReader, SlotPricingReader, ApplicationEventPublisher)`.\n- Split the existing `baseSlot()` helper into two:\n  - `private SlotSummaryDto baseSummary(SeatingMode mode, String seatMapId)` — returns a `SlotSummaryDto`.\n  - `private List<PricingTierDto> baseTiers()` — returns `List.of(new PricingTierDto(1L, \"Free\", new Money(BigDecimal.ZERO, \"INR\"), 10, \"FREE\", null, null, null, null))`.\n- Update all 4 test stubs: `when(schedulingSlotClient.getSlot(1L)).thenReturn(...)` → `when(slotSummaryReader.getSlotSummary(1L)).thenReturn(baseSummary(...))` plus `when(slotPricingReader.getSlotPricing(1L)).thenReturn(baseTiers())`.\n- All behavioral `verify(...)` assertions remain unchanged.\n\n---\n\n### Implementation Notes\n\n- Constructor injection only.\n- No new Maven dependencies (Jackson is already on the classpath;     "message": "Strong revision — this is now close to implementation-ready.  \nI see **2 mandatory corrections** to keep it aligned with the original task:\n\n1. **ArchUnit item must still be implemented explicitly**  \nYour revised plan says “no change required,” but the user task explicitly requires updating rules to encode the new allowance semantics.  \nEven if behavior is equivalent today, we should still update `BookingInventoryArchTest` to match requested intent.\n\n2. **Output contract mismatch (important)**  \nThe user asked for **full file paths + complete file contents + 2-line note per file**.  \nYour plan is implementation design, not the requested deliverable format.  \nSo next response should be structured as a file-by-file patch output (complete compilable files), not just plan prose.\n\nEverything else (especially accessor rename map, `SlotTicketSyncService` split-read fix, and Redis JSON cache rationale) is solid.\n\nIf you want, I can now produce the final **decision-complete implementation checklist mapped 1:1 to exact files to emit in that required output format** before coding.",
    "phase": "final_answer"
  }
}
```

Each file governs a specific concern. Read the ones that apply to your task.\n\n---\n\n## Reference Files\n\n| File | Governs |\n|---|---|\n| `PRODUCT.md` | The 7 modules, what each one owns, dependency order, inter-module event map, Eventbrite ACL facade names, hard architectural rules, and where new code goes. Read this before touching any module. |\n| `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` | Eventbrite integration architecture: ACL facades, all FR flows (planned vs. implemented), critical constraints (no user/order creation, no single-order cancel, no seat locking), workarounds. Read before designing any Eventbrite feature. |\n| `docs/MODULE_STRUCTURE_TEMPLATE.md` | Canonical folder and package layout for every module. The exact location of every layer: `api/controller/`, `api/dto/`, `domain/`, `service/`, `repository/`, `mapper/`, `event/published/`, `event/listener/`, `statemachine/`, `exception/`. Anti-patterns list. Read this before writing any class. |\n| `agents/SPEC_GENERATOR.md` | How to produce a `SPEC.md` for any feature. Eight confirmation-gated stages with module context and Eventbrite constraints built-in. Requirements → Domain Model → Architecture & File Structure → DB Schema → API → Business Logic → Error Handling → Tests. Read this before speccing any feature. |\n| `agents/CODING_AGENT.md` | Spring Boot 3.5.11 / Java 21 coding standards. Domain behaviour, service orchestration, mapper rules, controller rules, event rules, 10 Eventbrite ACL facades, inter-module communication (REST + Spring Events), admin/ special rules, state machine, Spring AI. Read this before writing any production code. |\n| `agents/TEST_AGENT.md` | Test agent responsibilities and best practices. Builds tests from `SPEC_*.md` Stage 8, executes tests, fixes test/code issues caused by behavior mismatches, and writes timestamped logs under `test_result_folder/`. Read this before writing or running tests. |\n| `docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md` | Four-layer test structure: `domain/` (unit), `service/` (Mockito), `api/` (@WebMvcTest), `arch/` (ArchUnit enforcing 10 hard rules + Eventbrite constraints). All rule examples, happy path, negative path, exception patterns. Read before writing any test. |\n| `mock-eventbrite-api/README.md` | **Mock Eventbrite Service:** Python FastAPI in-memory mock of Eventbrite API v3. Used for local development and testing. Exposes all 10 ACL facade endpoints without hitting production Eventbrite. See setup instructions below. |\n\n---\n\n## Module Quick Reference\n\nFull detail for each module is in `PRODUCT.md`. This table is a lookup only.\n\n| Module | Owns | Eventbrite ACL Facades Used |\n|---|---|---|\n| `discovery-catalog` | Events, Venues, Cities, catalog sync | `EbEventSyncService`, `EbTicketService` |\n| `scheduling` | Show slots, conflict validation | `EbEventSyncService` |\n| `identity` | Users, JWT, profiles, preferences, wallet | `EbAttendeeService` |\n| `booking-inventory` | Seats, SeatMap, Cart, Pricing, Seat Lock State Machine | `EbTicketService` |\n| `payments-ticketing` | Bookings, Payments, E-Tickets, Cancellations, Refunds, Wallet | `EbOrderService`, `EbAttendeeService`, `EbRefundService` |\n| `promotions` | Coupons, Promotions, Eligibility rules | `EbDiscountSyncService` |\n| `engagement` | Reviews, Moderation, AI Chatbot (`service/chatbot/`), RAG pipeline | `EbEventSyncService` |\n| `admin/` | No domain ownership. Orchestration and aggregation only. | — |\n| `shared/` | All external ACL facades, JWT, base classes, common DTOs, configs | — |\n\n---\n\n## Hard Rules — Never Violate\n\nCanonical numbering and wording are owned by `PRODUCT.md` Section 9. These shorthand rules apply to every task without exception.\n\n1. No module imports another module's `@Service`, `@Entity`, or `@Repository`.\n  - Exception: `admin/` MAY import module `@Service` beans for READ operations only; all writes MUST go through the owning module REST API.\n2. No module calls Eventbrite, Razorpay, or OpenAI HTTP directly — only through `shared/` ACL facades.\n3. ACL facade names are exactly as listed below — never invent new ones.\n4. Spring Events carry only primitives, IDs, enums, and value objects — never `@Entity` objects.\n5. One `GlobalExceptionHandler` in `shared/common/exception/` — never `@ControllerAdvice` in a module.\n6. All field mapping goes in `mapper/` using MapStruct — never inside a service or controller.\n7. `admin/` owns no `@Entity` and no database table.\n8. `shared/` has no dependency on any module.\n9. `app/` has zero business logic.\n10. Dependency direction is strictly downward — no circular imports between modules.\n\n---\n\n## Eventbrite ACL Facade Names (HARD RULE #3)\nRead the docs/eventbriteapiv3public.apib for detail API Endpoint with schema, Response, Request and errors\n\nAll 10 facades are in `shared/eventbrite/service/`. These are the ONLY things modules call for Eventbrite:\n\n1. **EbEventSyncService** — Create, update, publish, cancel events; pull org event catalog\n2. **EbVenueService** — Create and update venues; list org venues\n3. **EbScheduleService** — Create recurring event schedules and occurrences\n4. **EbTicketService** — Create/update ticket classes; manage inventory; check availability\n5. **EbCapacityService** — Retrieve and update event capacity tiers\n6. **EbOrderService** — Read orders post-checkout (NOT creation — JS SDK widget only)\n7. **EbAttendeeService** — List attendees; verify attendance for reviews\n8. **EbDiscountSyncService** — Create, update, delete discount codes\n9. **EbRefundService** — Read refund status only (NO submission API — workaround required)\n10. **EbWebhookService** — Mock/testing only; not used in production registration flow\n\n**See `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` for which module uses which facade and what gaps exist.**\n\n---\n\n## Module Dependency Order (HARD RULE #10)\n\n```\nshared/               <- foundational; no dependencies\n  |\n  v\ndiscovery-catalog    <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nscheduling           <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\nidentity             <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nbooking-inventory    <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\n  |\n  v\npayments-ticketing   <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\npromotions           <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nengagement           <- depends on shared + payments-ticketing + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nadmin/               <- thin orchestration; reads from all, writes via REST APIs\n  |\n  v\napp/                 <- entry point only; zero business logic\n```\n\n**Golden Rule:** Module at level N can depend on level N-1 or lower (or `shared/`) only. Never import from sibling or downstream modules.\n\n**See `PRODUCT.md` Section 2 for detailed rationale.**\n\n\n## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \"how do I do X\", \"find a skill for X\", \"is there a skill that can...\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. (file: /home/dell/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md)\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.",
    "truncation_policy": {
      "mode": "tokens",
      "limit": 10000
    }
  }
}

## Entry 551
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-04T13:57:59.522Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T13:57:59.522Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_n38dUGLSQlw6WYMSjMSqxET5",
    "output": "Chunk ID: b84545\nWall time: 0.0512 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 777\nOutput:\npackage com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.service;\n\nimport com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.service.client.SchedulingSlotClient;\nimport com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.service.client.SchedulingSlotResponse;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.common.domain.Money;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.common.event.published.SlotDraftCreatedEvent;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.common.event.published.TicketSyncCompletedEvent;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.common.event.published.TicketSyncFailedEvent;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.common.enums.SeatingMode;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.eventbrite.dto.request.EbInventoryTierRequest;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.eventbrite.dto.request.EbTicketClassRequest;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.eventbrite.exception.EbIntegrationException;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.eventbrite.service.EbTicketService;\nimport java.util.List;\nimport org.springframework.context.ApplicationEventPublisher;\nimport org.springframework.stereotype.Service;\n\n@Service\npublic class SlotTicketSyncService {\n\n    private final EbTicketService ebTicketService;\n    private final SchedulingSlotClient schedulingSlotClient;\n    private final ApplicationEventPublisher eventPublisher;\n\n    public SlotTicketSyncService(\n        EbTicketService ebTicketService,\n        SchedulingSlotClient schedulingSlotClient,\n        ApplicationEventPublisher eventPublisher\n    ) {\n        this.ebTicketService = ebTicketService;\n        this.schedulingSlotClient = schedulingSlotClient;\n        this.eventPublisher = eventPublisher;\n    }\n\n    public void syncTickets(SlotDraftCreatedEvent event) {\n        try {\n            SchedulingSlotResponse slot = schedulingSlotClient.getSlot(event.slotId());\n            List<EbTicketClassRequest> ticketClasses = slot.pricingTiers().stream()\n                .map(tier -> new EbTicketClassRequest(\n                    tier.name(),\n                    new Money(tier.priceAmount(), tier.currency()),\n                    tier.quota(),\n                    tier.tierType()\n                ))\n                .toList();\n\n            ebTicketService.createTicketClasses(event.ebEventId(), ticketClasses);\n\n            List<EbInventoryTierRequest> inventoryTiers = slot.pricingTiers().stream()\n                .map(tier -> new EbInventoryTierRequest(tier.name(), tier.quota()))\n                .toList();\n            ebTicketService.createInventoryTiers(event.ebEventId(), inventoryTiers);\n\n            if (slot.seatingMode() == SeatingMode.RESERVED && slot.sourceSeatMapId() != null) {\n                ebTicketService.copySeatMap(event.ebEventId(), slot.sourceSeatMapId());\n            }\n\n            eventPublisher.publishEvent(new TicketSyncCompletedEvent(event.slotId(), event.ebEventId()));\n        } catch (EbIntegrationException ex) {\n            eventPublisher.publishEvent(new TicketSyncFailedEvent(event.slotId(), event.ebEventId(), ex.getMessage()));\n            throw ex;\n        } catch (Exception ex) {\n            eventPublisher.publishEvent(new TicketSyncFailedEvent(event.slotId(), event.ebEventId(), ex.getMessage()));\n        }\n    }\n}\n"
  }
}
```

    "output": "Chunk ID: 1c0de0\nWall time: 0.0508 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 482\nOutput:\npackage com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.service;\n\nimport com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.service.client.SchedulingSlotClient;\nimport com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.service.client.SchedulingSlotResponse;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.common.exception.BaseException;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.common.exception.BusinessRuleException;\nimport java.util.Map;\nimport org.springframework.http.HttpStatus;\nimport org.springframework.stereotype.Service;\n\n@Service\npublic class SlotValidationService {\n\n    private final SchedulingSlotClient schedulingSlotClient;\n\n    public SlotValidationService(SchedulingSlotClient schedulingSlotClient) {\n        this.schedulingSlotClient = schedulingSlotClient;\n    }\n\n    public SchedulingSlotResponse requireActiveAndSynced(Long slotId) {\n        SchedulingSlotResponse slot = schedulingSlotClient.getSlot(slotId);\n        if (slot.ebEventId() == null || slot.ebEventId().isBlank()) {\n            throw new BaseException(\"Eventbrite event id is missing for slot\", \"EB_EVENT_NOT_SYNCED\", HttpStatus.SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE,\n                Map.of(\"slotId\", slotId)) {\n            };\n        }\n        if (!\"ACTIVE\".equalsIgnoreCase(slot.status())) {\n            throw new BaseException(\"Slot is not active\", \"SLOT_NOT_ACTIVE\", HttpStatus.CONFLICT, Map.of(\"currentStatus\", slot.status())) {\n            };\n        }\n        return slot;\n    }\n\n    public void ensureTierSynced(Long tierId, String ebTicketClassId) {\n        if (ebTicketClassId == null || ebTicketClassId.isBlank()) {\n            throw new BaseException(\"Tier not synced to Eventbrite\", \"TIER_NOT_SYNCED\", HttpStatus.SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE,\n                Map.of(\"tierId\", tierId)) {\n            };\n        }\n    }\n\n    public void ensureUserActive(String role) {\n        if (role == null || role.isBlank()) {\n            throw new BusinessRuleException(\"User context missing\", \"USER_CONTEXT_MISSING\");\n        }\n    }\n}\n"
  }
}

    "output": "Chunk ID: 8534ab\nWall time: 0.0506 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 1049\nOutput:\npackage com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.service;\n\nimport com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.domain.GroupDiscountRule;\nimport com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.domain.Seat;\nimport com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.repository.GroupDiscountRuleRepository;\nimport com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.repository.SeatRepository;\nimport com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.service.client.CatalogSeatLayoutClient;\nimport com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.service.client.CatalogSeatResponse;\nimport com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.service.client.SchedulingPricingTierResponse;\nimport com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.service.client.SchedulingSlotClient;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.common.enums.SeatingMode;\nimport java.math.BigDecimal;\nimport java.util.List;\nimport java.util.Locale;\nimport java.util.Map;\nimport java.util.function.Function;\nimport java.util.stream.Collectors;\nimport org.springframework.stereotype.Service;\nimport org.springframework.transaction.annotation.Transactional;\n\n@Service\npublic class SeatProvisioningService {\n\n    private final SeatRepository seatRepository;\n    private final GroupDiscountRuleRepository groupDiscountRuleRepository;\n    private final SchedulingSlotClient schedulingSlotClient;\n    private final CatalogSeatLayoutClient catalogSeatLayoutClient;\n    private final GaInventoryService gaInventoryService;\n\n    public SeatProvisioningService(\n        SeatRepository seatRepository,\n        GroupDiscountRuleRepository groupDiscountRuleRepository,\n        SchedulingSlotClient schedulingSlotClient,\n        CatalogSeatLayoutClient catalogSeatLayoutClient,\n        GaInventoryService gaInventoryService\n    ) {\n        this.seatRepository = seatRepository;\n        this.groupDiscountRuleRepository = groupDiscountRuleRepository;\n        this.schedulingSlotClient = schedulingSlotClient;\n        this.catalogSeatLayoutClient = catalogSeatLayoutClient;\n        this.gaInventoryService = gaInventoryService;\n    }\n\n    @Transactional\n    public void provision(Long slotId, Long venueId, SeatingMode seatingMode) {\n        List<SchedulingPricingTierResponse> tiers = schedulingSlotClient.getPricingTiers(slotId);\n        provisionGroupDiscountRules(slotId, tiers);\n\n        if (seatingMode == SeatingMode.GA) {\n            gaInventoryService.initCounters(slotId, tiers);\n            return;\n        }\n\n        if (seatRepository.existsByShowSlotId(slotId)) {\n            return;\n        }\n\n        Map<String, SchedulingPricingTierResponse> tierByName = tiers.stream()\n            .collect(Collectors.toMap(t -> normalize(t.name()), Function.identity(), (a, b) -> a));\n\n        List<CatalogSeatResponse> venueSeats = catalogSeatLayoutClient.getSeatLayout(venueId).seats();\n        List<Seat> seats = venueSeats.stream()\n            .map(venueSeat -> {\n                SchedulingPricingTierResponse tier = tierByName.get(normalize(venueSeat.tierName()));\n                if (tier == null) {\n                    return null;\n                }\n                return new Seat(\n                    slotId,\n                    tier.id(),\n                    tier.ebTicketClassId(),\n                    venueSeat.seatNumber(),\n                    venueSeat.rowLabel(),\n                    venueSeat.section()\n                );\n            })\n            .filter(java.util.Objects::nonNull)\n            .toList();\n\n        seatRepository.saveAll(seats);\n    }\n\n    private void provisionGroupDiscountRules(Long slotId, List<SchedulingPricingTierResponse> tiers) {\n        if (!groupDiscountRuleRepository.findByShowSlotId(slotId).isEmpty()) {\n            return;\n        }\n        List<GroupDiscountRule> rules = tiers.stream()\n            .map(tier -> new GroupDiscountRule(\n                slotId,\n                tier.id(),\n                tier.groupDiscountThreshold() == null ? Integer.MAX_VALUE : tier.groupDiscountThreshold(),\n                tier.groupDiscountPercent() == null ? BigDecimal.ZERO : tier.groupDiscountPercent()\n            ))\n            .toList();\n        groupDiscountRuleRepository.saveAll(rules);\n    }\n\n    private String normalize(String value) {\n        return value == null ? \"\" : value.toLowerCase(Locale.ROOT).trim();\n    }\n}\n"
  }
}

com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.service.client.SchedulingSlotClient;\nimport com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.service.client.SchedulingSlotResponse;\nimport
